_meta.ship_protocol: canonical filename rail_layer.json, CUMULATIVE full file each ship, real version in _meta.version

decisions_locked.D1: dual register — 2026 documentary + status_2060/derived_2060 DERIVED (derive_rail_2060.py is the only writer; guard recomputes and fails on mismatch). Adaptation premise + station persistence: terminal X-runs sever (stations kept on roster, rendered grayed); interior X-runs are kept through-running, stations as maintenance posts. Derived station names enter prose via tokens only.
decisions_locked.D2: intercity + regional passenger only; heritage/tourist IN; train ferries as declared ferry segments; bus links OUT; urban metro/commuter OUT
decisions_locked.D3: full resolution — every stop on included lines; tier-C lines declare roster:"partial" rather than fabricate halts
decisions_locked.D4: RL-### frozen on issue, never reassigned; groups keyed by operator/network
decisions_locked.D5: no workbook coupling — purely informational overlay

_meta.scope_defaults: existing = in passenger service or suspended-with-intact-track as of Jan 2026; conflict suspensions IN (Laurie 2026-07-20); abandoned OUT; freight lines IN only with scheduled passenger service

water_rule_exemptions.RL-334 seg0: Great Belt Fixed Link (Storebælt, ~18 km rail bridge/tunnel, Korsør–Nyborg) + Little Belt bridge — genuine fixed crossings between the Danish islands. Buffer not widened.
water_rule_exemptions.RL-356 seg0: Marmaray immersed-tube tunnel under the Bosphorus (rail). Genuine fixed crossing.
water_rule_exemptions.RL-362 seg0: Marmaray immersed-tube tunnel under the Bosphorus (rail). Genuine fixed crossing.

render_2060.truncated: The line runs as far as {NEW_TERMINI}. Beyond it the stations — {SEVERED_STOPS} — are kept on the books and the rails kept in the ground: if the country comes back, the railroad is already there.
render_2060.x-transit: This line is kept through-running across excluded country — a working crossing, not a ruin. {MAINTENANCE_STOPS} are maintained posts: water, crews, and a job for anyone who can keep a railroad alive in the dry.

_meta.city_ref_policy: Optional `city` on station nodes: C-series frozen ID where the stop serves an atlas city. Conservative name+proximity match (<30 km), guard-verified against the gazetteer. ID joins, never name joins.

Fort Lauderdale (Brightline) / Fort Lauderdale — distinct adjacent stations, deliberately not a junction
Miami Central (Brightline) / Miami — distinct stations, deliberately not a junction
Glen Rock-Main Line / Glen Rock-Boro Hall — two real stations 334 m apart on different lines, deliberately not a junction
London St Pancras / London King's Cross — adjacent termini across Euston Road, distinct stations, deliberately not a junction
Tri-Rail/Metrorail Transfer / Miami — adjacent platforms in one complex, distinct stops, deliberately not a junction
Chicago Ogilvie / Chicago Union — adjacent terminals across the river block, distinct stations, deliberately not a junction
Scarborough (ON) / Kennedy — distinct GO stations on different lines 402 m apart, deliberately not a junction
Haussmann-Saint-Lazare / Auber — distinct RER E/RER A stations in the Saint-Lazare complex, deliberately not a junction
Magenta / Paris Gare du Nord — distinct RER E station beside the mainline terminus, deliberately not a junction
Hachiōji / Keiō-Hachiōji — distinct JR and Keiō terminals ~600 m apart, deliberately not a junction
Keisei-Ueno / Ueno — distinct Keisei terminal beside JR Ueno, deliberately not a junction
Funabashi / Keisei-Funabashi — distinct JR and Keisei stations, deliberately not a junction
Glasgow Central / Glasgow Queen Street — Glasgow's two distinct main-line terminals ~600 m apart, deliberately not a junction
Chacarita / Federico Lacroze — distinct San Martín and Urquiza stations by the Chacarita cemetery, deliberately not a junction

Retiro complex: Buenos Aires Retiro / Retiro (Mitre) / Retiro (San Martín) / Retiro (Belgrano) are distinct adjacent terminals in the three-station Retiro complex (Mitre/San Martín/Belgrano), deliberately not junctions
Bilbao Abando / Bilbao Concordia — distinct adjacent Renfe (Abando) and FEVE narrow-gauge (Concordia) terminals, deliberately not a junction

Moscow terminals: Moscow Leningradsky / Moscow Kazansky / Moscow Yaroslavsky / Moscow Kursky / Moscow Belorussky / Moscow Savyolovsky are distinct adjacent terminals (the nine-terminal Moscow hub, three of them on Komsomolskaya square), deliberately not junctions
Ciudad Juárez / El Paso — distinct US-Mexico border-twin cities across the Rio Grande, meeting-but-separate (no through track), deliberately not a junction (Laurie border ruling)

derivation.rule: adaptation premise + station persistence (2026-07-20): terminal X-runs sever (stations kept on roster, grayed); interior X-runs are kept through-running with stations as maintenance posts
derivation.precision_caveat: zone test is nearest admin-1 centroid; border-hugging alignments are approximate

lines.name: Tōkaidō Shinkansen
lines.opened: 1964 — the first high-speed railway on Earth
lines.note: Tokyo to Shin-Osaka, 515 km, seventeen stations, and the template every other high-speed line copied. It parallels the old Tōkaidō road — the ancient way's post stations and these platforms serve the same corridor four centuries apart.
lines.research_note: Catenary end to end since 1964. Roster 17 stations, attested complete.

lines.name: Northeast Corridor (Boston–Washington)
lines.opened: assembled 1830s–1917; electrified New York–Washington 1935, to Boston 2000
lines.note: Boston to Washington through five of the continent's great cities, on rights-of-way assembled from railroads a century older than the trains that run on them.
lines.research_note: Busiest passenger railroad in the Americas; only fully electrified US intercity corridor (both 2026, expiry-tagged). Roster = intercity-served stops per D2; commuter overlays (MBTA, Metro-North, NJT, SEPTA, MARC) out of scope. Catenary. Roster attested complete.

lines.name: Settle–Carlisle Line
lines.opened: 1876 — the Midland Railway’s road over the Pennines; reprieved from closure 1989
lines.note: Seventy-three miles over the roof of England: Ribblehead Viaduct, Blea Moor Tunnel, and Dent station standing at 1,150 feet. Every stop is a working station; several reopened in 1986 after the community fought the closure and won.
lines.research_note: Dent = highest mainline station in England (stable, geographic). Diesel. Roster attested complete.

lines.name: The Ghan (Adelaide–Darwin)
lines.note: 2,979 km through the middle of the continent, and it calls at just four stations and two excursion sidings — the train sells an experience, not a timetable.
lines.note_2060: The train that ran to the Timor Sea now ends where the Mediterranean coast does. The alignment is kept — three thousand kilometres of standard gauge into the interior, grayed on this map: {SEVERED_STOPS} stand on the roster because the roadbed still stands under them.
lines.research_note: Named stops are the complete current passenger roster; all else is passing loops (Port Augusta, Tarcoola, Tennant Creek ruled out in rail_drops). Geometry follows the modern standard-gauge alignment, broadly the Stuart corridor north of Tarcoola, to shaping precision — tier-B basis. Diesel throughout.

lines.name: Villa San Giovanni–Messina rail ferry
lines.opened: 1899 — through carriages have crossed the Strait for over a century
lines.note: InterCity coaches from the mainland are shunted aboard, cross the Strait of Messina, and continue on Sicilian rails — through carriages have made this crossing for over a century.
lines.research_note: Last great European train ferry in daily mixed passenger service (2026, hedged). Ferry leg modeled as declared crossing per D2; rail approaches catenary. Roster attested complete.

lines.name: Ffestiniog Railway
lines.opened: 1836 — the oldest operating railway company in the world
lines.note: Thirteen and a half miles of 1 ft 11½ in gauge from Porthmadog harbour up to the slate town of Blaenau Ffestiniog, including the Dduallt spiral, built by volunteers in the 1960s to climb around a reservoir that drowned the old alignment. Still worked by the company's own locomotives, some approaching their 160th birthday.
lines.research_note: Dduallt = only railway spiral in Britain (stable). Steam traction; heritage class. Roster attested complete incl. request halts; Campbell's Platform (private) excluded.

lines.name: Fianarantsoa–Côte Est (FCE)
lines.opened: 1936 — built with rail salvaged from Alsace after the First World War
lines.note: 163 km from the highlands down the escarpment to the Indian Ocean at Manakara, through country with no parallel road for much of the way — for the villages between, this train IS the road, carrying bananas and coffee out and everything else in.
lines.research_note: ~17 stations on the line; 7 attested here, town precision. Roster declared partial per charter §5 — missing halts are real and unfabricated, awaiting a source. Diesel railcar service. Tier C.

lines.name: City of New Orleans (Chicago–New Orleans)
lines.opened: Illinois Central corridor, 1850s; the streamliner 1947; Amtrak 1971
lines.note: The Illinois Central main line south — the corridor of the Great Migration run in reverse, nine hundred miles from Chicago down the Mississippi's shoulder to the Gulf.
lines.note_2060: South of {NEW_TERMINI} the corridor of the Great Migration goes quiet. The stations toward the delta — {SEVERED_STOPS} — are kept on the roster, grayed: the rails are held as far as the river towns can hold them.
lines.research_note: Full Amtrak roster attested. Diesel. House pattern locked here: copy never states where the 2060 line ends — the derivation does.

lines.name: Southwest Chief (Chicago–Los Angeles)
lines.opened: ATSF transcon, 1880s; the Super Chief 1936; Amtrak 1971
lines.note: The Santa Fe transcon: Chicago to Los Angeles over Raton Pass and down the old Route 66 country — Las Vegas that isn't the one you think, Winslow of the song, the Mojave at night.
lines.note_2060: The transcon is kept open — the working land bridge between the eastern and Pacific settlement belts. The maintenance posts at {MAINTENANCE_STOPS} are crewed year-round: hard postings, real wages, and the only lights for a hundred miles.
lines.research_note: Full Amtrak roster attested, 31 stops. Diesel. Williams Junction dropped 2018 (shuttle ended) — logged in rail_drops.

lines.name: Empire Builder (Chicago–Seattle)
lines.note: Chicago to Puget Sound along the Hi-Line — the northernmost transcontinental, hugging the Canadian border across the plains and threading Marias Pass under the shoulder of Glacier. For the towns strung along the top of Montana it is the only thing on rails that stops.
lines.research_note: Seattle main stem; Portland section (Spokane split) deferred to a later batch as its own entry. Roster attested against current Amtrak service. Small-town coords to town precision — tier B. Diesel.

lines.name: California Zephyr (Chicago–Emeryville)
lines.note: Chicago to the Bay through the middle of everything: the Nebraska plains at night, the Front Range at breakfast, the Moffat Tunnel, Glenwood Canyon where the track and the Colorado River share the same shelf, the Great Basin, and Donner Pass coming down into California.
lines.research_note: Reputation: widely called the most scenic train in America (2026). Roster attested. Rockies/Sierra geometry hand-placed at corridor bends; plains densified — tier B. Diesel.
lines.note_2060: The train still climbs the Rockies; it is the country past them that emptied. {MAINTENANCE_STOPS} hold the crossing open — high posts, long blocks, and the Colorado for company through the canyons.

lines.name: Sunset Limited (New Orleans–Los Angeles)
lines.opened: Southern Pacific 1894 — the oldest named train in the United States
lines.note: The southern transcontinental: the Gulf coast, the Texas hill towns, the long Rio Grande country past Alpine and Marfa's lights, then the low desert crossings at Yuma where the railroad found the one soft place in the Colorado's banks.
lines.research_note: Roster attested (tri-weekly). Tier B. Diesel. Derivation history: B04 kept crossing -> B05 collapsed (stopless east flank) -> geometry v3 (Balcones annex, Laurie ruling) restored the live east flank at San Antonio; desert maintained again, Gulf approach remains ruled-out drowned tail.
lines.note_2060: The oldest named train is whole again from the Hill Country west: the desert crossing is held by {MAINTENANCE_STOPS}, and only the drowned approach — {SEVERED_STOPS} — waits grayed at the Gulf end.

lines.name: Coast Starlight (Seattle–Los Angeles)
lines.opened: assembled from SP/GN coastal services; Amtrak 1971
lines.note: Puget Sound to Los Angeles down the whole Pacific slope: the Willamette Valley, the Cascade crossing above Odell Lake, Mount Shasta out the right-hand window at dawn, and the last hundred miles run on the beach itself along the Gaviota coast, closer to the surf than the highway ever gets.
lines.research_note: Roster attested. Cascade and Gaviota geometry hand-placed; valley runs densified. Tier B. Diesel.
lines.note_2060: The coast route lost only its valley miles: {MAINTENANCE_STOPS} hold the crossing of the emptied Central Valley, and everything on the water side runs as it always has.

lines.name: Crescent (New York–New Orleans)
lines.opened: Southern Railway 1891; the Crescent Limited 1925; Amtrak 1979
lines.note: New York to New Orleans down the spine of the piedmont — the mill towns and college towns of Virginia and the Carolinas, Atlanta at midnight, then the long southwestern slant across Alabama and Mississippi to the Gulf. A train that still does what the interstates never quite managed: stop everywhere that matters in between.
lines.research_note: Roster attested. Shares NEC trackage NY–Washington; those stops listed once here at Crescent calling points. Tier B. Diesel south of Washington (catenary under wires north — mixed traction declared). Derivation note: expect truncation in the Deep South against X004.
lines.note_2060: The piedmont held; the Gulf slope did not. The train runs the mill-town spine as far as {NEW_TERMINI}; the stations on the long southwestern slant — {SEVERED_STOPS} — are kept grayed, rails in the ground.

lines.name: East Coast Main Line (London–Edinburgh)
lines.note: King's Cross to Waverley in four and a half hours: the flat-out racing ground south of York where Mallard took the steam record, Durham cathedral filling the window on its peninsula, and the final run along the cliffs at Berwick where the line crosses the Tweed on Stephenson's Royal Border Bridge.
lines.research_note: Roster = intercity-served principal stations (RL-002 precedent); the line hosts dense commuter service south of Peterborough, out of scope per D2. Catenary. Roster attested against LNER calling points.

lines.name: West Coast Main Line (London–Glasgow)
lines.opened: L&B/Grand Junction 1837–38 core; electrified to Glasgow 1974
lines.note: Euston to Glasgow over Shap and Beattock — the two climbs that broke steam locomotives for a century and now barely register — express, freight, and everything else sharing the same four tracks.
lines.research_note: Oldest trunk railway in the world by its southern miles (historical, stable); busiest mixed-traffic main line in Europe (2026, expiry-tagged). Roster = principal intercity calling points, main stem; Birmingham/Manchester loops deferred. Commuter service south of Milton Keynes out of scope per D2. Catenary.

lines.name: Paris–Lyon–Marseille (LGV Sud-Est / Méditerranée)
lines.opened: LGV Sud-Est 1981 — Europe’s first high-speed line; Méditerranée 2001
lines.note: Paris to the Mediterranean in three hours: the line that proved the Shinkansen idea worked in Europe, and the 2001 extension that put Marseille a morning away from the capital. The Rhône valley at 300 km/h, with the Alps standing off to the east the whole way down.
lines.research_note: Roster = LGV through-stations on the Paris–Marseille axis; classic-line TGV branches deferred. Catenary. 'Europe's first high-speed line' — historical, stable.

lines.name: Cologne–Frankfurt high-speed line
lines.note: The line that straightened the Rhine: two hundred kilometres of 300 km/h running beside the autobahn, cutting Cologne–Frankfurt from over two hours along the river to just over one. The gradients are motorway gradients — only the ICE 3 could climb them, so the line and the train were designed as one machine.
lines.research_note: Roster = high-speed line stations; the classic Rhine valley line (the scenic one) is a separate future entry. Catenary.

lines.name: Milan–Rome–Naples (Alta Velocità)
lines.opened: Direttissima 1977–92 (Europe’s first new-build fast line); AV completed 2009
lines.note: The Italian spine at speed: Milan to Naples in just over four hours, down the Po plain, through the Apennine base tunnels to Florence, and along the old Direttissima alignment to Rome — a line Italy started building before the TGV existed and finished a generation later.
lines.research_note: Roster = AV through-stations on the spine; Turin and Venice arms deferred. Catenary. Historical primacy claims verified against Direttissima opening dates (1977 first section).

lines.name: San’yō Shinkansen (Shin-Osaka–Hakata)
lines.note: The Tōkaidō's continuation west: Kobe, Himeji under its white castle, Hiroshima, then under the Kanmon Strait to Kyushu — more than half the route in tunnel, which is what it costs to run flat and straight through the Chūgoku mountains.
lines.research_note: Roster 19 stations, attested complete. Catenary. Junction with RL-001 at Shin-Osaka — coordinate-identical by construction, guard-verified.

lines.name: Tōhoku Shinkansen (Tokyo–Shin-Aomori)
lines.note: North out of Tokyo up the whole length of Honshu's spine — 320 km/h across the Sendai plain, into country that gets snowier and emptier until the line dives toward the strait at Aomori.
lines.research_note: Roster 23 stations, attested complete (all-stops Yamabiko/Hayabusa union). Catenary. Junction with RL-001 at Tokyo — coordinate-identical, guard-verified. 'Fastest in Japan' — 2026, expiry-tagged.

lines.name: Howrah–New Delhi (Grand Chord corridor)
lines.note: Calcutta to Delhi across the whole Gangetic plain — the imperial trunk turned national artery: the coalfields, Gaya, the great river crossings, Kanpur's mills, and the plain that never quite ends until Delhi rises out of it.
lines.research_note: Heaviest passenger corridor on Earth by ridership (2026, expiry-tagged). Roster = Rajdhani-class principal calling points (RL-002/RL-015 precedent); hundreds of stopping services out of D2 scope. City precision — tier B. Catenary.
lines.note_2060: The trunk is kept whole: {MAINTENANCE_STOPS} hold the plain as posts, and the three great routes still meet under the empty capital.

lines.name: Mumbai–New Delhi (Western corridor)
lines.note: Bombay to Delhi up the western side of the country: out of the coastal wet at Surat and Vadodara, over the Malwa plateau at Ratlam, down through Kota on the Chambal, and across Rajasthan's edge to the capital — sixteen hours that cross three climates.
lines.research_note: Roster = Mumbai Rajdhani calling points. City precision — tier B. Catenary. Kota–Delhi geometry via Sawai Madhopur/Mathura shaping.
lines.note_2060: Kept through to Delhi: the lowland and Chambal miles — {MAINTENANCE_STOPS} — are crossed as posts, and the western trunk still reaches the meeting of the lines.

lines.name: Chennai–New Delhi (Grand Trunk corridor)
lines.note: The Grand Trunk route: Madras to Delhi through the middle of the peninsula — up the Coromandel edge to Vijayawada, across the Deccan through Warangal and Nagpur (the zero milestone of India, near enough), Bhopal, the fortress country at Jhansi and Gwalior, Agra with the Taj off the left side, and in.
lines.research_note: Roster = GT/Rajdhani-class principal calling points. City precision — tier B. Catenary. Chennai–Vijayawada shaping via Nellore/Ongole; Warangal–Nagpur via Balharshah; Nagpur–Bhopal via Itarsi.
lines.note_2060: Kept through the fortress country: {MAINTENANCE_STOPS} stand as posts under the old capitals, and the Grand Trunk still arrives at Delhi.

lines.name: Konkan corridor (Mumbai–Mangalore)
lines.opened: 1998 — the hardest railway India ever built
lines.note: Down the wet green shelf between the Western Ghats and the Arabian Sea: two thousand bridges and ninety tunnels in seven hundred and forty kilometres, through country the British surveyed and gave up on. Goa in the middle, Mangalore at the bottom, and the monsoon the whole way.
lines.research_note: Roster = principal express calling points on the Konkan alignment plus Mumbai approach. City precision — tier B. Catenary (electrified 2020s). Bridge/tunnel counts are Konkan Railway's own figures.

lines.name: Howrah–Chennai (East Coast corridor)
lines.note: The Coromandel run: Calcutta to Madras down the eastern seaboard — Kharagpur, the Odisha coast at Bhubaneswar, the harbour at Visakhapatnam, the Godavari crossed at Rajahmundry, and into Vijayawada where the trunk routes braid.
lines.research_note: Kharagpur platform-length record retired by Gorakhpur 2013 — removed from copy per superlative ruling. Roster = Coromandel-class principal calling points. City precision — tier B. Catenary. Shares Vijayawada–Chennai trackage with RL-024 (registry-derived junctions).
lines.note_2060: The Coromandel is kept whole: the Bengal approach — {MAINTENANCE_STOPS} — is held as the crossing north, where the coalfield line still comes down to meet it.

lines.name: Beijing–Shanghai high-speed railway
lines.note: Capital to coast in four and a half hours: down the North China Plain, across the Yellow River at Jinan, past Qufu — Confucius gets his own station — over the Yangtze at Nanjing on the Dashengguan bridge, and into the delta.
lines.research_note: Busiest high-speed line in the world (2026, expiry-tagged, ridership basis). Full station roster attested (23). Major stations station precision, minor to town — tier B. Catenary.
lines.note_2060: The line runs from the delta to the Yellow River: beyond {NEW_TERMINI} the northern plain stations wait grayed, and the Huai crossing is held by {MAINTENANCE_STOPS}.

lines.name: Beijing–Guangzhou high-speed railway
lines.opened: completed 2012 — the longest high-speed line in the world at opening
lines.note: 2,298 km straight down the middle of China: the North China Plain, the Yellow River at Zhengzhou, the Yangtze at Wuhan, the lake country at Changsha, and the Nanling mountains threaded in tunnel after tunnel down to the Pearl River delta. Eight hours, top to bottom, through more people than live in most continents.
lines.research_note: Roster = principal calling points (the all-stations set exceeds 35; principal set per D2 intercity scoping, research-noted). City/town precision — tier B. Catenary. 'Longest at opening' — historical, stable.
lines.note_2060: Cut at both ends, alive in the middle: between {NEW_TERMINI} the interior spine still runs, its long blocks held by {MAINTENANCE_STOPS}, the two great termini grayed at either end.

lines.name: Trans-Siberian Railway (Moscow–Vladivostok)
lines.opened: 1891–1916 — the longest railway in the world since the day it closed its last gap
lines.note: Nine thousand two hundred and eighty-nine kilometres and seven days end to end: the Volga cities, the Urals at Yekaterinburg, the West Siberian plain where the horizon is the only landmark, the Yenisei at Krasnoyarsk, half a day along the southern shore of Baikal, the Shilka and Amur country, and down the Ussuri to the Pacific. Still the spine of a continent.
lines.research_note: Roster = Rossiya-class principal calling points (~30); the line hosts hundreds of local services out of D2 scope. City precision — tier B. Catenary (electrified throughout 2002). Baikal south-shore and Ussuri geometry hand-placed; steppe runs densified. 'Longest railway' — stable since 1916.

lines.name: Texas Eagle (Chicago–San Antonio)
lines.note: Chicago to San Antonio by way of the whole middle: the Illinois prairie towns, the Gateway Arch filling the window at St. Louis, Arkansas timber country, Texarkana straddling its own state line, then Dallas, Fort Worth, and down the Balcones edge through Austin to the Alamo city — where through cars join the Sunset for the west.
lines.research_note: Roster attested (daily). City precision — tier B. Diesel. Authored on Laurie's ruling (2026-07-20); healed same day by geometry v3's Balcones annex (R015): Dallas, Fort Worth, Austin, San Antonio live. The San Antonio junction with RL-012 gives the Sunset its live east flank.

lines.name: Lake Shore Limited (New York–Chicago)
lines.note: New York to Chicago the flat way: up the Hudson under the Palisades, west along the old Water Level Route through the Erie Canal towns, the lake shore at Cleveland in the small hours, and into Chicago with the steel country behind.
lines.research_note: Roster attested (NY stem; Boston section via Albany deferred as own entry). Tier B. Diesel east of Albany under wire — mixed declared.

lines.name: Capitol Limited (Washington–Chicago)
lines.opened: B&O 1830s alignment; the Capitol Limited 1923; Amtrak 1981
lines.note: The old B&O main west: out of Washington through Harpers Ferry where the rivers meet, over the Alleghenies at Sand Patch, Pittsburgh at midnight, and the lake-plain run to Chicago on the first railroad main line America built.
lines.research_note: Roster attested. Tier B. Diesel. Shares Cleveland–Chicago trackage with RL-031 — coordinate-identical stations, registry-bound.

lines.name: Cardinal (New York–Chicago via the New River Gorge)
lines.note: The slow, beautiful way west: down the NEC, through the Blue Ridge at Charlottesville, then the whole New River Gorge — Hinton, Prince, Thurmond and its ghost-town depot — coal country, Cincinnati, and the Indiana flat to Chicago. Three days a week and worth arranging a life around.
lines.research_note: Roster attested (tri-weekly). Tier B. Mixed traction (NEC under wire). White Sulphur Springs stop = the Greenbrier — cross-ref hot springs line HS-800 scope query.

lines.name: Silver Meteor (New York–Miami)
lines.opened: Seaboard Air Line 1939 — the first Florida streamliner
lines.note: The Florida train: down the seaboard through the Carolinas' tobacco towns, the low country at Charleston and Savannah, then the long peninsula — Jacksonville, the citrus middle, the lake at Sebring — and the gold coast stations one after another to Miami.
lines.research_note: Roster attested (Silver Star and Auto Train deferred as own entries). Tier B. Diesel (mixed under NEC wire). Derivation interest: Miami is ledger-evacuated — expect the peninsula to gray.
lines.note_2060: The seaboard holds; past {NEW_TERMINI} the whole peninsula waits grayed — {SEVERED_STOPS} — rails on the ridge, water rising on both sides.

lines.name: Carolinian (New York–Charlotte)
lines.note: The daytime Piedmont train: the seaboard to Richmond, then inland through the tobacco and university towns — Raleigh, Durham, Burlington — to join the Crescent's spine at Greensboro and finish in Charlotte.
lines.research_note: Roster attested. Tier B. Mixed traction. Greensboro–Charlotte coordinate-shared with RL-014 — registry-bound.

lines.name: Empire Service (New York–Niagara Falls)
lines.note: The state's own railroad: the Hudson to Albany, then the canal-line towns strung west — Amsterdam, Utica, Rome, Syracuse, Rochester — ending above the gorge at Niagara Falls.
lines.research_note: Roster attested (corridor all-stops union). Tier B. Diesel. Shares Hudson/canal trackage with RL-031 — registry-bound.

lines.name: Vermonter (Washington–St. Albans)
lines.note: The Connecticut River train: the NEC to New Haven, up the river valley through Springfield and the college towns, then Vermont proper — Brattleboro, Montpelier, the Winooski crossing — to within sight of the border at St. Albans.
lines.research_note: Roster attested to principal + Vermont all-stops; CT valley intermediate halts (Berlin/Meriden/Wallingford/Windsor) attested. Tier B. Mixed traction.

lines.name: Downeaster (Boston–Brunswick)
lines.note: North out of Boston through the mill towns and into Maine: Exeter, Dover, the beach halt at Old Orchard in summer, Portland's working waterfront, and the Bowdoin end of the line at Brunswick.
lines.research_note: Roster attested complete incl. seasonal Old Orchard Beach. Tier A (well-known compact corridor). Diesel.

lines.name: Amtrak Cascades (Eugene–Vancouver BC)
lines.note: The Northwest corridor: the Willamette Valley, Portland, the Sound at Tacoma and Seattle, then the shoreline run north under the Chuckanut cliffs to Bellingham and across the border to Vancouver.
lines.research_note: Roster attested (corridor all-stops union). Tier B. Diesel. Shares Eugene–Everett trackage with RL-013/RL-010 — registry-bound. International terminus in scope (D2 silent on borders; timetable decides).

lines.name: Pacific Surfliner (San Diego–San Luis Obispo)
lines.note: The everyday train at volume: the beach line north from San Diego, surf out the left windows through Oceanside and San Clemente, the LA basin, then the second act — the Ventura coast and the Gaviota cliffs to San Luis Obispo.
lines.research_note: Roster attested (all-stops union; some stops served by subset of frequencies). Tier B. Diesel. Shares LA–SLO trackage with RL-013 — registry-bound.

lines.name: San Joaquins (Oakland–Bakersfield)
lines.note: The valley train: out of the Bay through the delta at Antioch, then straight down the great flat middle — Stockton, Modesto, Merced, Fresno — farm towns and heat shimmer all the way to the end of track at Bakersfield.
lines.research_note: Roster attested (Oakland stem; Sacramento stem deferred). Tier B. Diesel. Derivation interest: the Central Valley X ring — expect the valley to gray.
lines.note_2060: The Bay end lives to the delta; beyond {NEW_TERMINI} the valley stations — {SEVERED_STOPS} — stand grayed in the heat, the rails as straight as ever underneath it.

lines.name: Capitol Corridor (San Jose–Auburn)
lines.note: The Bay's commuter-intercity hybrid: around the南 bay from San Jose, up the East Bay shore, through the Carquinez narrows at Martinez, Davis and Sacramento, and into the Sierra foothills at Auburn.
lines.research_note: Roster attested. Tier B. Diesel. Shares Bay trackage with RL-011/RL-041 — registry-bound.
lines.note_2060: The corridor is kept into the foothills: {MAINTENANCE_STOPS} stay lit because the mountain crossing runs through them anyway.

lines.name: Heartland Flyer (Fort Worth–Oklahoma City)
lines.note: North across the Red River: the little cross-timbers towns — Gainesville, Ardmore, Pauls Valley — then Norman and the state capital, one train a day each way and the only passenger rail Oklahoma has.
lines.research_note: Roster attested complete. Tier A (compact, well-known). Diesel. Fort Worth junction with RL-030 — registry-bound.

lines.name: Missouri River Runner (St. Louis–Kansas City)
lines.note: Across Missouri along the river: the wine-town halt at Hermann, the capitol dome at Jefferson City, Sedalia of the ragtime festivals, and in along the bluffs to Kansas City's grand Union Station.
lines.research_note: Roster attested complete. Tier A. Diesel. Junctions: St. Louis (RL-030), Kansas City (RL-009) — registry-bound.

lines.name: Hiawatha (Chicago–Milwaukee)
lines.opened: Milwaukee Road Hiawatha lineage 1935; corridor service under Amtrak
lines.note: The corridor sprint: eighty-six miles, seven round trips a day, the lakefront out one side and the glacial farm country out the other — the Midwest's case that short trains run often beat any highway.
lines.research_note: Roster attested complete. Tier A. Diesel. Chicago–Milwaukee trackage shared with RL-010 — registry-bound.

lines.name: Brightline (Miami–Orlando)
lines.opened: 2018 (Miami–WPB); through to Orlando 2023 — the first private US intercity railroad in a century
lines.note: The private experiment that worked: bright yellow trains up the Florida East Coast corridor from Miami's downtown vertical station, then the new-build 125 mph cutoff inland to the airport terminal at Orlando.
lines.research_note: Roster attested as of Jan 2026 (Stuart infill unverified in-service by cutoff — logged in drops, revisit). Tier B. Diesel. Brightline stations are DISTINCT from adjacent Amtrak stations (WPB/FTL) — near-miss exemptions declared, deliberately not junctions.
lines.note_2060: Every stop grayed end to end: the newest railroad in the layer, banked whole against a Florida that kept more of its water than its people.

lines.name: Alaska Railroad (Anchorage–Fairbanks)
lines.opened: completed 1923 — the President drove the golden spike at Nenana
lines.note: The canonically alone railroad: no connection to any other line on the continent, north from tidewater at Anchorage up the Susitna, past Denali when the mountain is out, and over the interior hills to Fairbanks.
lines.research_note: Roster = Denali Star principal stops (flagstop territory between attested locally, out of roster scope — noted). Tier B. Diesel. Isolated network by design: no junctions expected, ever.

lines.name: Empire Builder — Portland section (Spokane–Portland)
lines.note: The Builder's other half: split at Spokane in the dark, then down the dry side of the Columbia — Wishram under the basalt cliffs, the gorge narrowing all the way — into Portland by the water-level route the mountains never touch.
lines.research_note: Deferred stub from B04, cleared. Roster attested. Tier B. Diesel. Spokane and Vancouver/Portland stations coordinate-shared with RL-010/RL-013/RL-039 — registry-bound.

lines.name: Lake Shore Limited — Boston section (Boston–Albany)
lines.note: The Berkshire leg: out through Framingham and Worcester, over the hills at Pittsfield on the old Boston & Albany, joining the Water Level Route at Albany.
lines.research_note: Deferred stub from B12, cleared. Roster attested complete. Tier A. Diesel. Albany and Springfield junctions registry-bound.

lines.name: Silver Star (New York–Miami via Columbia & Tampa)
lines.note: The Meteor's inland sibling: the seaboard to Richmond, then the old Seaboard main through the sandhills — Southern Pines, Columbia — to Savannah, down to Jacksonville, and the double act in Florida: into Tampa, reverse, and across to the gold coast.
lines.research_note: Roster attested to high confidence EXCEPT: Jesup, Palatka, Winter Haven, Delray Beach presence on the Star (vs Meteor-only) unverified — omitted pending source, logged. Tampa reversal modeled as two segments through Lakeland. Tier B. Mixed traction.
lines.note_2060: Both Silvers tell the same story now: live to the seaboard's end, and past {NEW_TERMINI} the sandhills route runs grayed to the peninsula — {SEVERED_STOPS} on the books, salt in the ballast at the far end.

lines.name: Auto Train (Lorton–Sanford)
lines.note: The train you drive onto: cars and their people loaded at Lorton, unloaded at Sanford, nothing in between but the night and nine hundred miles of the Atlantic seaboard.
lines.research_note: Two-stop roster attested trivially complete. Tier A. Diesel. Nonstop by design — shaping only between termini.

lines.name: San Joaquins — Sacramento stem (Sacramento–Stockton)
lines.note: The short northern arm of the valley train: Sacramento down through Lodi's vineyards to Stockton, where it joins the main valley run.
lines.research_note: Deferred stub cleared. Roster attested. Tier A. Diesel. Stockton modeled at the RL-041 coordinate (two physical stations ~1 km apart collapsed to city resolution — simplification declared; junction binding intended).

lines.name: Cape Town–Johannesburg (Karoo main line)
lines.note: The long diagonal across the Karoo: out of the Cape through the Hex River Pass, then the high dry middle — Beaufort West, De Aar of the junctions, Kimberley of the diamonds — and down onto the Rand at Johannesburg.
lines.research_note: Principal-stop roster; service history includes suspensions with intact track (IN per charter §6). Tier B. Mixed traction (3 kV DC sections + diesel gaps declared as mixed).

lines.name: Madaraka Express (Nairobi–Mombasa SGR)
lines.opened: 2017 — the standard-gauge rebuild of the Lunatic Line’s corridor
lines.note: Nairobi to the coast in five hours where the metre gauge took a night: down through the Tsavo country the first railway made famous, Voi at the halfway water, and out of the hills to the Indian Ocean at Mombasa.
lines.research_note: Inter-county stopping roster attested. Tier B. Diesel. Group kenya-railways.

lines.name: Addis Ababa–Djibouti Railway
lines.opened: 2018 — electric from the highlands to the sea
lines.note: The electric line down the escarpment: out of Addis at altitude, the Awash gorge, Dire Dawa where the old French railway made its town, and the desert descent to the port at Djibouti — a landlocked country's road to the ocean, under wire the whole way.
lines.research_note: Principal-stop roster attested. Tier B. Catenary (notable: fully electric new-build).

lines.name: Al Boraq (Tangier–Casablanca)
lines.note: Tangier to Casablanca in just over two hours: the new-build 320 km/h line down the Atlantic plain to Kenitra, then the upgraded classic route through the capital to Casablanca.
lines.research_note: Roster attested. Tier B. Catenary. 'Africa's first high-speed' — historical, stable.

lines.name: TAZARA (Dar es Salaam–Kapiri Mposhi)
lines.opened: 1975 — built by 50,000 workers in five years
lines.note: The freedom railway: Dar es Salaam up through the Udzungwa escarpments, Mbeya in the highlands, over the border at Tunduma, and across the Zambian plateau to the end of the line at Kapiri Mposhi — built to give the copper a route to the sea that owed nothing to the south.
lines.research_note: Tier C, roster PARTIAL: the line has ~90 stations; the nine named are attested principal stops at town precision. Service intermittent with intact track (IN per §6). Diesel.

lines.name: Buenos Aires–Mar del Plata
lines.opened: Ferrocarril del Sud 1886; the summer train ever since
lines.note: The holiday line: out of Constitución across the flat pampa — Chascomús on its lagoon, Dolores, the grass running to every horizon — and into Mar del Plata with half the city's summer aboard.
lines.research_note: Roster = current stopping pattern, attested. Tier B. Diesel.

lines.name: Buenos Aires–Rosario–Córdoba
lines.note: The long slow train up the Paraná shore to Rosario and across the pampa gradual to Córdoba — hours the buses beat and riders keep choosing anyway, for the fare and the room and the way the country goes by.
lines.research_note: Tier C, roster PARTIAL: intermediate halts (Zárate, San Pedro, Cañada de Gómez and others) not individually attested for the current timetable — principal stops only, town precision. Diesel.

lines.name: EFE Santiago–Chillán
lines.opened: Chilean longitudinal railway; electric services renewed 2020s
lines.note: The central valley under the Andes wall: Rancagua, the wine towns, Talca, and the fast electric units running the flat straight south to Chillán.
lines.research_note: Roster attested (express pattern). Tier B. Catenary (3 kV DC).

lines.name: Cusco–Machu Picchu (Aguas Calientes)
lines.note: The narrow gauge down the Urubamba: switchbacks out of the Cusco valley, Ollantaytambo among its terraces, then the gorge — river on one side, cloud forest walls on the other — to the roadless town below the citadel.
lines.research_note: Tourist/heritage class per D2. Roster = principal passenger stations. Tier B. Diesel. Aguas Calientes is road-free: this line is the town's only wheeled access.

lines.name: Vitória–Minas passenger (Belo Horizonte–Vitória)
lines.opened: EFVM 1904; the daily passenger train has never stopped running
lines.note: Brazil's surviving daily long-distance train: down the Rio Doce valley from Belo Horizonte to the sea at Vitória, thirteen hours among the ore trains, serving valley towns nothing else serves.
lines.research_note: Tier C, roster PARTIAL: the train makes ~30 stops; four principal named at town precision. Diesel.

lines.name: Adirondack (New York–Montréal)
lines.note: Up the Hudson and then the length of Lake Champlain on the water's very edge — Ticonderoga, Westport, Port Kent for the ferry — the border at Rouses Point, and across the flat Richelieu country into Montréal.
lines.research_note: Roster attested incl. seasonal Port Kent. Tier B. Diesel. Shares Hudson trackage with RL-031/036 — registry-bound.

lines.name: Maple Leaf (New York–Toronto)
lines.note: The Empire route to the falls, the gorge crossing, and then the Niagara fruit belt — St. Catharines, the lake appearing at Grimsby — around the head of Ontario into Toronto Union's great hall.
lines.research_note: Roster attested. Tier B. Diesel. NYP–Niagara Falls (NY) trackage shared with RL-036 — registry-bound; crew and flag change at the border, one train number throughout.

lines.name: Ethan Allen Express (New York–Burlington)
lines.opened: the Ethan Allen 1996; extended to Burlington 2022
lines.note: The other Vermont train: the Hudson to Albany, the spa town at Saratoga, then east over the border of the marble country to Rutland and north up the Champlain valley to the waterfront at Burlington.
lines.research_note: Roster attested. Tier B. Diesel. Albany/Saratoga/Fort Edward/Whitehall shared with RL-063 — registry-bound. [Rhinecliff + Hudson added — Hudson Valley stops (Laurie), coord-matched to RL-036/063; derive state-supported defense.]

lines.name: Pennsylvanian (New York–Pittsburgh)
lines.note: The Pennsylvania Railroad's main stem: the electrified racetrack to Harrisburg, then the mountain division — the Juniata valley, Altoona of the shops, around Horseshoe Curve with the whole valley below — and down the Conemaugh to Pittsburgh.
lines.research_note: Roster attested. Tier B. Mixed traction (catenary NYP–Harrisburg, diesel west). Pittsburgh junction with RL-032 — registry-bound.

lines.name: Keystone Service (New York–Harrisburg)
lines.note: The electric corridor west of Philadelphia: the Main Line suburbs, the Amish farm country past Lancaster, the Susquehanna arrival at Harrisburg — 110 mph under wire on a line older than the state's highways.
lines.research_note: Roster attested complete (corridor all-stops union). Tier A. Catenary throughout.

lines.name: VIA Corridor East (Québec–Montréal–Ottawa–Toronto)
lines.note: Canada's main street: the Château skyline receding from Gare du Palais, the south shore to Montréal, then the capital and the long lakeshore string — Kingston's limestone, Belleville, Cobourg — into Toronto with the tower out the left window.
lines.research_note: Roster = corridor calling-point union via the Ottawa routing; the direct Montréal–Cornwall–Kingston lakeshore variant deferred as own entry. Tier B. Diesel.

lines.name: VIA Corridor West (Toronto–Windsor)
lines.note: Southwestern Ontario: around the lakehead through the fruit belt, Brantford of the telephone, London in its forest of a city, and the flat tomato country down to the border at Windsor with Detroit's towers across the river.
lines.research_note: Roster attested (Sarnia branch deferred). Tier B. Diesel. Toronto/Oakville/Aldershot shared with RL-064 — registry-bound.

lines.name: The Canadian (Toronto–Vancouver)
lines.note: Four nights across the country: the Shield's rock and lake maze north of Superior, the prairies opening at Winnipeg, Saskatoon and the parkland, the Rockies arriving at Jasper, and the canyons — Thompson, then Fraser — funnelling everything down to tidewater at Vancouver.
lines.research_note: Roster = current principal calling points (twice-weekly consist; extensive flagstop territory noted, out of roster scope). Tier B. Diesel. Vancouver junction with RL-039 — registry-bound.

lines.name: The Ocean (Montréal–Halifax)
lines.opened: ICR 1876; the Ocean is North America’s oldest continuously named train
lines.note: Down the St. Lawrence's south shore into the dark, the Matapédia valley threading the Gaspé hills at dawn, Chaleur Bay at Campbellton, New Brunswick's long woods, and the tide country to Halifax — the Atlantic end of the national system.
lines.research_note: Roster attested. Tier B. Diesel. Montréal/Saint-Lambert/Drummondville shared with RL-063/068 — registry-bound. 'Oldest continuously named' — historical, stable, in opened field.

lines.name: Winnipeg–Churchill
lines.opened: Hudson Bay Railway 1929; the polar bear train
lines.note: North until the trees give out: the parkland, The Pas, the muskeg miles on ground that moves under the track, Thompson's nickel country, and the last tundra stretch to Churchill on Hudson Bay — no road goes there; this train is the road.
lines.research_note: Tier C, roster PARTIAL: the service makes dozens of flagstops; eight principal stations named, town precision. Thompson served via a reversal spur, modeled inline (simplification declared). Diesel.

lines.name: LIRR Main Line (New York–Ronkonkoma–Greenport)
lines.note: The island's spine: out through Jamaica's great interlocking, the Hempstead Plains towns, Ronkonkoma at the island's waist, then the single-track scoot through the farm-and-vineyard North Fork to the harbor at Greenport.
lines.research_note: All-stops roster (Ronkonkoma service + Greenport scoot). Tier B, village precision. Third rail to Ronkonkoma; diesel scoot east — mixed.

lines.name: LIRR Montauk Branch (New York–Montauk)
lines.note: The Hamptons train: the South Shore to Babylon, then the pine barrens and the summer names one by one — Southampton, Bridgehampton, East Hampton — to the lighthouse end of everything at Montauk.
lines.research_note: All-stops roster east of Babylon (express west, per service pattern). Tier B. Third rail to Babylon, diesel east — mixed.

lines.name: LIRR Babylon Branch
lines.note: The South Shore locals: Rockville Centre to Babylon town by town, electric territory end to end, the ocean a few blocks south the whole way.
lines.research_note: Busiest LIRR electric territory (2026, expiry-tagged). All-stops roster. Tier B. Third rail.

lines.name: LIRR Port Jefferson Branch
lines.note: The North Shore: Huntington's harbor towns, the Nissequogue country at Smithtown, Stony Brook for the university, and the ferry landing at Port Jefferson.
lines.research_note: All-stops roster. Tier B. Third rail to Huntington, diesel east — mixed.

lines.name: LIRR Port Washington Branch
lines.note: The one that skips Jamaica: Flushing, the bay towns of Little Neck and Great Neck, and Manhasset's peninsula down to the town dock at Port Washington.
lines.research_note: All-stops roster. Tier B. Third rail.

lines.name: LIRR Hempstead Branch
lines.note: The old plains line: Queens Village and Floral Park, Garden City's planned green streets, and the county seat at Hempstead.
lines.research_note: All-stops roster. Tier B. Third rail.

lines.name: LIRR Far Rockaway Branch
lines.note: Down through the Five Towns to the barrier edge: Valley Stream, the Hewletts and Woodmere, and the ocean-side terminal at Far Rockaway.
lines.research_note: All-stops roster. Tier B. Third rail. Barrier-lowland geography — SLR interest noted.

lines.name: LIRR Long Beach Branch
lines.note: Across the marsh islands on the trestles to the city by the sea — the only LIRR branch that ends on a barrier island proper.
lines.research_note: All-stops roster. Tier B. Third rail. THE SLR showcase branch: trestle-and-barrier alignment.

lines.name: LIRR West Hempstead Branch
lines.note: The quiet one: five villages between Valley Stream and West Hempstead, a railroad at neighborhood scale that still connects three municipalities.
lines.research_note: All-stops roster. Tier B, village precision. Third rail.

lines.name: LIRR Oyster Bay Branch
lines.note: The Gold Coast local: Roslyn's mill pond, Sea Cliff on its bluff, Glen Cove, and Theodore Roosevelt's home stop at Oyster Bay.
lines.research_note: All-stops roster. Tier B, village precision. Third rail to East Williston, diesel beyond — mixed.

lines.name: LIRR Atlantic Branch (Brooklyn–Jamaica)
lines.note: Brooklyn's own terminal: Atlantic Avenue under Flatbush, East New York, and the run to Jamaica that knits the borough into the island system.
lines.research_note: All-stops roster. Tier B. Third rail.

lines.name: Metro-North Hudson Line (Grand Central–Poughkeepsie)
lines.note: The river line: under Park Avenue, out along the Hudson's very edge past the Palisades, the prison at Ossining, the Highlands squeeze at Cold Spring and Garrison, Beacon under its mountain, and Poughkeepsie's great bridge overhead.
lines.research_note: All-stops roster. Tier B. Third rail to Croton-Harmon, diesel north — mixed. Croton-Harmon/Yonkers/Poughkeepsie coordinate-shared with Amtrak lines — registry-bound.

lines.name: Metro-North Harlem Line (Grand Central–Wassaic)
lines.note: Up the middle: the Bronx locals, Scarsdale and White Plains, the reservoir country past Katonah, Brewster, and the long single track of the Harlem Valley to the hamlet end of the line at Wassaic.
lines.research_note: All-stops roster incl. the Appalachian Trail flag halt. Tier B. Third rail to Southeast, diesel north — mixed.

lines.name: Metro-North New Haven Line (Grand Central–New Haven)
lines.note: The megalopolis made visible: forty miles of shoreline towns each with its green and its station — Rye, Greenwich, Stamford's towers, the Norwalks, Fairfield — under wires strung a century ago, into New Haven where everything meets.
lines.research_note: All-stops roster. Tier B. Catenary. New Rochelle/Stamford/Bridgeport/New Haven coordinate-shared with the NEC — registry-bound. Shoreline alignment: SLR interest.

lines.name: Metro-North New Canaan Branch
lines.note: Four stops off the main at Stamford, climbing the ridge to New Canaan.
lines.research_note: All-stops roster. Tier B. Catenary.

lines.name: Metro-North Danbury Branch
lines.note: The Norwalk River valley single track: Wilton, the Branchville junction that no longer junctions, Bethel, and the hat city at Danbury.
lines.research_note: All-stops roster. Tier B. Diesel.

lines.name: Metro-North Waterbury Branch
lines.note: Up the Naugatuck: the brass-valley towns in a row — Derby, Ansonia, Seymour, Naugatuck — to Waterbury under its clock tower.
lines.research_note: All-stops roster. Tier B. Diesel.

lines.name: Metro-North Port Jervis Line (Hoboken–Port Jervis)
lines.note: The west-of-Hudson orphan: out through Bergen County to Suffern, the Ramapo pass, Tuxedo and Harriman, the Moodna country, Middletown, and the Delaware at Port Jervis where three states meet.
lines.research_note: All-stops roster (through Bergen County per service pattern). Tier B. Diesel. NJT-operated for MNR — group MTA per network identity.

lines.name: NJT Northeast Corridor Line (New York–Trenton)
lines.note: The locals on the racetrack: every town the expresses blur past — Elizabeth, Rahway, Metuchen, New Brunswick — the corridor as its residents actually ride it.
lines.research_note: All-stops roster. Tier B. Catenary. Coordinate-shared throughout with RL-002 — registry-bound.

lines.name: NJT North Jersey Coast Line (New York–Bay Head)
lines.note: Down the Shore by rail: the Amboys, Red Bank on the Navesink, then the beach towns in a string — Long Branch, Asbury Park, Belmar, Spring Lake — to the end of track at Bay Head, salt air on the platforms most of the way.
lines.research_note: All-stops roster incl. seasonal Monmouth Park. Tier B. Catenary to Long Branch, diesel south — mixed. Barrier-adjacent lowland alignment: primary SLR-interest line of the NJ network.

lines.name: NJT Morristown Line (New York–Hackettstown)
lines.note: The Lackawanna main: the Oranges climbing the ridge, Summit at the top, Madison and Morristown's greens, the lake country past Dover, and the hills to Hackettstown.
lines.research_note: All-stops roster. Tier B. Catenary to Dover, diesel west — mixed.

lines.name: NJT Gladstone Branch
lines.note: The single-track idyll off Summit: the Watchung towns, the Great Swamp's edge at Millington and Lyons, Bernardsville, and the horse country end at Gladstone.
lines.research_note: All-stops roster. Tier B, village precision. Catenary.

lines.name: NJT Montclair-Boonton Line (New York–Hackettstown)
lines.note: Six stations up Montclair's own ridge, then the Boonton line proper — Little Falls, the Towaco curves, Boonton over its gorge — joining the Morristown Line's west end at Denville.
lines.research_note: All-stops roster. Tier B. Catenary to Montclair State, diesel west — mixed. Denville–Hackettstown coordinate-shared with RL-093 — registry-bound.

lines.name: NJT Raritan Valley Line (Newark–High Bridge)
lines.note: The old Jersey Central: Cranford and Westfield's downtowns built around their stations, Plainfield, Somerville, and the Raritan's south branch country out to High Bridge.
lines.research_note: All-stops roster. Tier B. Diesel.

lines.name: NJT Main Line (Hoboken–Suffern)
lines.note: The Erie's old main up the Passaic: Passaic and Paterson of the mills, the Ridgewood junction country, and the Ramapo gap at Suffern where the New York trains take over.
lines.research_note: All-stops roster. Tier B. Diesel. Ridgewood/Suffern coordinate-shared with RL-090 — registry-bound.

lines.name: NJT Bergen County Line (Hoboken–Ridgewood)
lines.note: The other way north: Rutherford, Garfield, the Fair Lawn stations including Radburn's planned-town platform, rejoining the Main Line at Ridgewood.
lines.research_note: All-stops roster. Tier B. Diesel.

lines.name: NJT Pascack Valley Line (Hoboken–Spring Valley)
lines.note: The valley locals: Hackensack's two stations, the Pascack towns one after another — Westwood, Hillsdale, Park Ridge — over the state line to Spring Valley.
lines.research_note: All-stops roster. Tier B, village precision. Diesel.

lines.name: NJT Atlantic City Line (Philadelphia–Atlantic City)
lines.note: Across the Pinelands to the boardwalk: Lindenwold, Hammonton in the blueberry country, Egg Harbor, and over the meadows causeway to the barrier city itself.
lines.research_note: All-stops roster. Tier B. Diesel. Terminal on a barrier island across tidal meadows: SLR-interest line #2 of the NJ network.

lines.name: NJT Princeton Branch (the Dinky)
lines.note: Two stops, one car, five minutes: the junction to the university, the shortest scheduled journey in the layer and among the oldest in continuous service.
lines.research_note: Two-stop roster, trivially complete. Tier A. Catenary. 'Shortest in the layer' — internal fact, self-verifying against the file.

lines.name: KTX Gyeongbu (Seoul–Busan)
lines.note: The peninsula's spine at 300 km/h: out of Seoul under the mountains, Daejeon at the junction of everything, Daegu in its basin, and down the Nakdong to the harbor amphitheater of Busan.
lines.research_note: Roster = KTX calling-point union. Tier B. Catenary.

lines.name: KTX Honam (Yongsan–Mokpo)
lines.opened: Honam high-speed line 2015; to Mokpo 2025 works
lines.note: The southwest line: the rice plain at Iksan, Gwangju of the uprisings and the gardens, Naju's old town, and the islands beginning at Mokpo where the land runs out into the archipelago.
lines.research_note: Roster = KTX calling-point union. Tier B. Catenary. Osong/Gwangmyeong shared with RL-102 — registry-bound.

lines.name: Taiwan High Speed Rail (Nangang–Zuoying)
lines.opened: 2007 — the west coast compressed to ninety minutes
lines.note: Down the crowded western plain: Taipei's basin, the science city at Hsinchu, Taichung, the temple country of Chiayi and Tainan, and into Zuoying with Kaohsiung's harbor cranes beyond.
lines.research_note: Full station roster attested. Tier B. Catenary.

lines.name: North–South Railway (Hanoi–Ho Chi Minh City)
lines.opened: 1936 through-line; rebuilt after 1975 as the Reunification Express
lines.note: The whole country on one metre-gauge line: the Red River delta, the long central coast with the sea at the window for days — Hue's citadel, the Hai Van pass above the clouds, Nha Trang's bay — and down into the Mekong lowlands to Saigon.
lines.research_note: Roster = Reunification Express principal calling points. Tier B. Diesel. Hai Van shaping hand-placed; coastal alignment throughout — X002 derivation interest maximal.
lines.note_2060: The line that sewed the country together is grayed end to end: {DROWNED_STOPS} — the sea took the coast road's twin. Kept on the books entire.

lines.name: SRT Northern Line (Bangkok–Chiang Mai)
lines.note: Out of the delta and up the country: Ayutthaya's ruins beside the track, the central plain, Phitsanulok, then the hills — the Khun Tan tunnel under its shrine, Lamphun's longans — and down into the old northern capital at Chiang Mai.
lines.research_note: Roster = express calling points. Tier B. Diesel. Bangkok delta X002 derivation interest.
lines.note_2060: The hills held and the delta did not: the line lives above {NEW_TERMINI}; below it, {DROWNED_STOPS} gray into the floodplain.

lines.name: SRT Southern Line (Bangkok–Padang Besar)
lines.note: Down the isthmus: Hua Hin's royal seaside, the narrow waist at Prachuap with Myanmar's hills in sight of the gulf, Chumphon, Surat Thani for the islands, and the peninsula's spine to Hat Yai and the border interchange at Padang Besar.
lines.research_note: Roster = express calling points. Tier B. Diesel. Padang Besar coordinate-shared with RL-108 — international junction, registry-bound.
lines.note_2060: Grayed from the delta to the border: {DROWNED_STOPS} — the isthmus route waits under a different tide line.

lines.name: KTM ETS (Padang Besar–KL Sentral–Gemas)
lines.note: The Malayan west coast under wire: Alor Setar's paddies, Butterworth for the Penang ferry, Taiping's rain, the limestone karsts announcing Ipoh, and down the electrified main to Kuala Lumpur and the meter-gauge junction at Gemas.
lines.research_note: Roster = ETS calling-point union. Tier B. Catenary.
lines.note_2060: North of {NEW_TERMINI} the frontier stop — {DROWNED_STOPS} — waits grayed; south of it the electric main runs whole.

lines.name: Java North Line (Jakarta–Surabaya)
lines.note: The pantura line: out of Jakarta's sinking coastal plain, Cirebon, the long shore run to Semarang, and inland across the delta country to Surabaya — a hundred and forty million people's island stitched together at grade.
lines.research_note: Roster = Argo-class principal calling points (all-stations locals out of pattern scope, noted). Tier B. Diesel. Jakarta subsidence + X002: derivation interest maximal. 'Busiest island' — demographic fact, stable framing, research-tagged.
lines.note_2060: The north shore line grayed entire — {DROWNED_STOPS} — Jakarta first among them, sinking before the sea even rose. Kept on the books.

lines.name: AVE Madrid–Barcelona
lines.note: The meseta at 310 km/h: out of Atocha's palm house, the Aragonese steppe past Zaragoza, Lleida's fruit country, and down through the coastal hills into Sants.
lines.research_note: Roster = AVE calling-point union. Tier B. Catenary.

lines.name: AVE Madrid–Sevilla
lines.opened: 1992 — Spain’s first high-speed line, built for the Expo
lines.note: The original AVE: through the Despeñaperros gap in the Sierra Morena, Córdoba with the Mezquita ten minutes' walk from the platform, and into Sevilla's Santa Justa.
lines.research_note: Roster attested. Tier B. Catenary. 'First Spanish HSL' — historical, in opened field.

lines.name: Alfa Pendular (Lisboa–Porto)
lines.note: Portugal's main line: out of Oriente's white canopies, the Tagus valley, Coimbra on its hill, the Aveiro lagoon country, and across the Douro into Campanhã.
lines.research_note: Roster = Alfa calling points. Tier B. Catenary.

lines.name: Amsterdam–Brussels axis
lines.opened: Hollandsche IJzeren 1839 lineage; the Benelux axis
lines.note: The low-country main: Schiphol under its runways, Rotterdam's rebuilt glass hall, the Moerdijk crossing, Antwerp's cathedral of a station, and into Brussels-South — four capitals of trade on one line, most of it below the high-tide line.
lines.research_note: Roster = IC calling-point union. Tier B. Catenary. Derivation interest: the Randstad's below-sea-level geography vs the atlas's zone map.

lines.name: SJ X2000 (Stockholm–Göteborg)
lines.note: The western main across the lake country: Södertälje, the plains at Katrineholm and Skövde, Alingsås, and down the Göta valley into Gothenburg's harbor city.
lines.research_note: Roster = X2000 calling-point union. Tier B. Catenary.

lines.name: Bergensbanen (Oslo–Bergen)
lines.opened: 1909 — the high crossing of the Hardangervidda
lines.note: Over the roof of Norway: the Hallingdal valley climbing stop by stop, Geilo, then the treeless plateau — Finse at 1,222 metres, ice on the lakes in June — Myrdal above the Flåm cleft, and the long descent through Voss to the fjord city.
lines.research_note: Roster = express calling points. Tier B. Catenary. Finse altitude is a stable geographic fact, retained in copy.

lines.name: Öresund axis (København–Stockholm)
lines.note: Across the strait on the bridge-and-tunnel, then the length of southern Sweden: Lund's spires, the Småland forests at Alvesta and Nässjö, the plain cities of Linköping and Norrköping, and into Stockholm between its waters.
lines.research_note: Roster = X2000 CPH–Stockholm calling-point union. Tier B. Catenary. Öresund crossing passes the water rule within coastal buffer (fixed link, no exemption needed). Stockholm/Södertälje shared with RL-114 — registry-bound.

lines.name: Gotthard axis (Zürich–Milano)
lines.opened: Gotthard 1882; Base Tunnel 2016 — 57 km under the massif
lines.note: The Alps removed from the equation: Zug and the lake at Arth-Goldau, then fifty-seven kilometres of mountain passed in twenty minutes, the sudden Ticino light at Bellinzona, Lugano's bay, and down past Como into Milano Centrale.
lines.research_note: Roster = EC calling-point union via the base tunnel (portal shaping at Erstfeld/Bodio). Tier B. Catenary. Milano junction with RL-019 — registry-bound.

lines.name: ÖBB Westbahn axis (Wien–Salzburg–Innsbruck)
lines.note: Austria end to end: the Wachau's edge past St. Pölten, Linz on the Danube, the lake district at Attnang, Salzburg under its fortress, then the German corridor through Rosenheim and up the Inn — Kufstein, Wörgl — into Innsbruck ringed by its wall of mountains.
lines.research_note: Roster = Railjet calling-point union; Deutsches Eck transit shaped through Rosenheim. Tier B. Catenary.

lines.name: VR main line (Helsinki–Oulu)
lines.note: North through the lake plateau: Tampere between its rapids, the Ostrobothnian flats at Seinäjoki, the coast regained at Kokkola, and Oulu at the top of the gulf — white nights on the platforms half the year.
lines.research_note: Roster = IC calling points. Tier B. Catenary.

lines.name: Eurostar (London–Paris)
lines.note: Under the sea between two capitals: out of St Pancras's blue vault, the Kent high-speed line, twenty minutes of Channel, and the beet plains of Picardy at 300 km/h into the Gare du Nord.
lines.research_note: Roster = core calling pattern; Ashford/Ebbsfleet intermediate service suspended with intact platforms (IN per §6 would apply to lines, not stops — omitted from roster as unserved, logged). Channel Tunnel passes the water rule within the coastal buffer (fixed link). Tier B. Catenary.

lines.name: MBTA Providence Line
lines.opened: 19th c. heritage lines, modern state operation
lines.note: Boston to Providence on the NEC's own rails — two state capitals forty minutes apart, the definition of the megalopolis at commuter fare.
lines.research_note: All-stops roster (Stoughton spur deferred). Tier B. Catenary under the NEC wire. Providence/Route 128 coordinate-shared with RL-002 — registry-bound.

lines.name: MBTA Framingham/Worcester Line
lines.opened: 19th c. heritage lines, modern state operation
lines.note: The Boston & Albany inner miles: the Newtons in a row, the Wellesleys in a row, Natick and Framingham, and the climb to Worcester.
lines.research_note: All-stops roster. Tier B. Diesel. Framingham/Worcester shared with RL-049 — registry-bound.

lines.name: MBTA Fitchburg Line
lines.opened: 19th c. heritage lines, modern state operation
lines.note: Thoreau's railroad: past Walden at Concord, the old mill towns — Ayer, Shirley — and up to Fitchburg and the Wachusett extension.
lines.research_note: All-stops roster. Tier B. Diesel.

lines.name: MBTA Lowell Line
lines.opened: 19th c. heritage lines, modern state operation
lines.note: The Boston & Lowell of 1835 — among the oldest commuter runs anywhere — Winchester, Woburn, and the canal city at the end.
lines.research_note: All-stops roster. Tier B. Diesel. Anderson/Woburn coordinate-shared with RL-038.

lines.name: MBTA Haverhill Line
lines.opened: 19th c. heritage lines, modern state operation
lines.note: Up through the Melroses and Reading, Andover, and the Merrimack mill cities — Lawrence, Haverhill — the immigrant-industry corridor in commuter form.
lines.research_note: All-stops roster. Tier B. Diesel. Haverhill shared with RL-038.

lines.name: MBTA Newburyport Line
lines.opened: 19th c. heritage lines, modern state operation
lines.note: The North Shore: Lynn, witch-and-maritime Salem, Beverly, Ipswich's clam flats, and the Merrimack mouth at Newburyport.
lines.research_note: All-stops roster. Tier B. Diesel.

lines.name: MBTA Rockport Line
lines.opened: 19th c. heritage lines, modern state operation
lines.note: Around Cape Ann: the Beverly shore, Manchester-by-the-Sea, Gloucester of the fleet, and granite Rockport at land's end.
lines.research_note: All-stops roster. Tier B. Diesel. Trunk shared with RL-126 — registry-bound.

lines.name: MBTA Franklin Line
lines.opened: 19th c. heritage lines, modern state operation
lines.note: Southwest through the Norwoods and Walpole to Franklin and the 495 belt.
lines.research_note: All-stops roster. Tier B. Diesel.

lines.name: MBTA Needham Line
lines.opened: 19th c. heritage lines, modern state operation
lines.note: Through the streetcar-suburb hills of Roslindale and West Roxbury to the town of Needham — the shortest full line on the system, and still a two-municipality artery.
lines.research_note: All-stops roster. Tier B. Diesel.

lines.name: MBTA Fall River Line (South Coast Rail)
lines.opened: 19th c. heritage lines, modern state operation
lines.note: The 2025 restoration: Brockton's shoe city, Middleborough, and new track to the old mill port at Fall River — trains again after sixty years.
lines.research_note: All-stops roster (South Coast Phase 1 pattern). Tier B. Diesel.

lines.name: MBTA New Bedford Line (South Coast Rail)
lines.opened: 19th c. heritage lines, modern state operation
lines.note: The other arm of the restoration: the same trunk south, then east to the whaling city — New Bedford back on the passenger map.
lines.research_note: All-stops roster. Tier B. Diesel. Trunk shared with RL-130 — registry-bound.

lines.name: MBTA Kingston Line
lines.opened: 19th c. heritage lines, modern state operation
lines.note: The Old Colony south: Weymouth and the Bridgewaters' cousins — Abington, Whitman, Hanson — to Kingston by Plymouth's shore.
lines.research_note: All-stops roster. Tier B. Diesel.

lines.name: MBTA Greenbush Line
lines.opened: 19th c. heritage lines, modern state operation
lines.note: The shore branch: Hingham harbor country, Cohasset, North Scituate, and the marsh-edge terminal at Greenbush.
lines.research_note: All-stops roster. Tier B. Diesel. Coastal-marsh alignment — SLR interest.

lines.name: MARC Penn Line (Washington–Perryville)
lines.opened: 19th c. heritage lines, modern state operation
lines.note: The workhorse between the capitals-of-sorts: DC to Baltimore in forty minutes on the NEC, and the quieter miles beyond to the Susquehanna at Perryville.
lines.research_note: All-stops roster. Tier B. Mixed traction. DC/BWI/Baltimore/Aberdeen coordinate-shared with RL-002 — registry-bound.

lines.name: MARC Camden Line
lines.opened: 19th c. heritage lines, modern state operation
lines.note: The B&O original: College Park and Laurel on the oldest railroad alignment in the country, into the ballpark warehouse at Camden Yards.
lines.research_note: All-stops roster. Tier B. Diesel.

lines.name: MARC Brunswick Line (Washington–Martinsburg)
lines.opened: 19th c. heritage lines, modern state operation
lines.note: The B&O west: the Montgomery towns, Point of Rocks under its witch-hat station, Harpers Ferry in the gap, and over the ridge to Martinsburg.
lines.research_note: All-stops roster. Tier B. Diesel. Rockville/Harpers Ferry/Martinsburg coordinate-shared with RL-032 — registry-bound.

lines.name: VRE Fredericksburg Line
lines.opened: 19th c. heritage lines, modern state operation
lines.note: Down the RF&P: Alexandria, the Potomac towns, Quantico between the base and the river, and Fredericksburg with the Spotsylvania tail.
lines.research_note: All-stops roster. Tier B. Diesel. Alexandria/Quantico/Fredericksburg/Lorton coordinate-shared with RL-014/035/051 — registry-bound (Lorton: the Auto Train finally has a neighbor).

lines.name: VRE Manassas Line
lines.opened: 19th c. heritage lines, modern state operation
lines.note: The Crescent's inner miles at commuter frequency: Burke Centre, Manassas Park, and the battlefield junction town itself, out to Broad Run.
lines.research_note: All-stops roster. Tier B. Diesel. Alexandria/Manassas coordinate-shared with RL-014 — registry-bound.

lines.name: Caltrain (San Francisco–Gilroy)
lines.note: The peninsula's spine, electric at last: 22nd Street under Potrero Hill, the peninsula towns each with a station a mile apart, Palo Alto and the valley of the garages, San Jose, and the quiet tail through garlic country to Gilroy.
lines.research_note: All-stops roster. Tier B. Electrified SF–Tamien 2024, diesel south — mixed. Santa Clara / San Jose Diridon coordinate-shared with RL-013/042 — registry-bound.

lines.name: Metrolink San Bernardino Line
lines.note: The San Gabriel Valley in beads: El Monte, Covina, the Claremont colleges, and the Inland Empire cities to San Bernardino's restored depot.
lines.research_note: All-stops roster. Tier B. Diesel. San Bernardino coordinate-shared with RL-009 — registry-bound.

lines.name: Metrolink Antelope Valley Line
lines.note: Over the mountains to the high desert: Burbank, the Santa Clarita valley, the Soledad Canyon grades past Acton, and out onto the Antelope Valley floor at Palmdale and Lancaster.
lines.research_note: All-stops roster. Tier B. Diesel. Derivation interest: the high-desert end meets X004.

lines.name: Metrolink Ventura County Line
lines.note: The Valley's own service west: Van Nuys, Northridge, the Simi grade, and the coastal plain to East Ventura alongside the Surfliner.
lines.research_note: All-stops roster. Tier B. Diesel. Shares Surfliner stations coordinate-identical — registry-bound.

lines.name: Metrolink Orange County Line
lines.note: Down the old Santa Fe Surf Line at commuter frequency: Fullerton, Anaheim, Santa Ana, the canyon at San Juan Capistrano, and the beach at San Clemente to Oceanside.
lines.research_note: All-stops roster. Tier B. Diesel. Surfliner-shared stations registry-bound; the San Clemente bluff alignment is a standing coastal-erosion story — SLR interest.

lines.name: Metrolink Inland Empire–Orange County Line
lines.note: The suburb-to-suburb original: San Bernardino and Riverside to the Orange County job centers without touching Los Angeles — the first US commuter line built on that bet.
lines.research_note: All-stops roster. Tier B. Diesel. 'First suburb-to-suburb' — historical, stable, kept here in research.

lines.name: Metrolink Riverside Line
lines.note: The UP corridor east: Industry's warehouses, downtown Pomona on the Sunset's own platform, East Ontario, and along the river reach to Riverside.
lines.research_note: All-stops roster. Tier B. Diesel. Pomona coordinate-shared with RL-012 — registry-bound.

lines.name: Metrolink 91/Perris Valley Line
lines.note: The 91 corridor and beyond: Fullerton, the Coronas, Riverside, and the 2016 extension up the Perris Valley to March Field and Perris.
lines.research_note: All-stops roster. Tier B. Diesel.

lines.name: Sounder S Line (Seattle–Lakewood)
lines.note: The Green and Puyallup valleys at rush hour: Kent, Auburn, Sumner's daffodil flats, the Dome at Tacoma, and Lakewood at the end of the wire-less line.
lines.research_note: All-stops roster. Tier B. Diesel. Seattle/Tacoma coordinate-shared with RL-013/039 (Tacoma Dome collapsed to city resolution, declared) — registry-bound.

lines.name: Sounder N Line (Seattle–Everett)
lines.note: The shoreline commute: Elliott Bay, the Edmonds ferry slip, Mukilteo, and Everett along track that runs on the beach itself.
lines.research_note: All-stops roster. Tier B. Diesel. Beach-level alignment — SLR interest.

lines.name: ACE (Stockton–San Jose)
lines.note: Over the Altamont against the grain: the valley towns, Tracy, the pass with its windmills, the Tri-Valley, and around the bay's foot into San Jose.
lines.research_note: All-stops roster. Tier B. Diesel. Stockton at city resolution (RL-041 precedent); Fremont/Santa Clara/Diridon registry-bound. Derivation interest: the valley end meets the San Joaquin ruling.
lines.note_2060: The pass is kept from the living side: the line runs to {NEW_TERMINI} at the valley's very edge, and beyond it the delta stations — {SEVERED_STOPS} — stand grayed under the ruling that closed the valley.

lines.name: Coaster (Oceanside–San Diego)
lines.note: The lagoon-hopping locals: both Carlsbads, Encinitas, the Del Mar bluff country at Sorrento Valley, and into Santa Fe Depot by the harbor.
lines.research_note: All-stops roster. Tier B. Diesel. Surfliner-shared stations registry-bound; Del Mar bluffs — SLR interest.

lines.name: Tri-Rail (Miami Airport–Mangonia Park)
lines.note: The gold coast's third railroad: airport to Mangonia Park up the CSX corridor, inland twin to the coastal lines, every county seat of the drowned shore on its roster.
lines.research_note: All-stops roster. Tier B. Diesel. Boca Raton (Tri-Rail) is a distinct station from Brightline's; Amtrak-shared stops registry-bound. Ships to gray per the florida-gold-coast ruling — mapped because Laurie wants the drowned mapped.
lines.note_2060: Grayed end to end with its coastal twins: {SEVERED_STOPS} — three railroads' worth of platforms on one drowned shore, kept on the books together.

lines.name: FrontRunner (Ogden–Provo)
lines.note: The Wasatch Front in one line: Ogden to Provo down the bench, every valley city on the way, the lake on one side and the wall of mountains on the other.
lines.research_note: All-stops roster. Tier B. Diesel. SLC/Provo coordinate-shared with RL-011 — registry-bound. Ships to gray per the Wasatch canon (SLC ledgered): the corridor mapped station by station because Laurie wants the emptied mapped.
lines.note_2060: The Front mapped station by station, all of it grayed: {SEVERED_STOPS} — the bench line under the empty wall, its two great stops still crewed for the crossing that passes through.

lines.name: SEPTA Paoli/Thorndale Line
lines.note: The Main Line itself: the colleges and the old money stops in a row — Bryn Mawr, Villanova, Radnor — Paoli, and the Chester Valley to Thorndale.
lines.research_note: All-stops roster. Tier B. Catenary. Paoli/Exton/Downingtown coordinate-shared with RL-066/067 — registry-bound.

lines.name: SEPTA Trenton Line
lines.note: The NEC locals' local: the river-ward neighborhoods, Cornwells Heights, Bristol on the Delaware, Levittown, and the state line at Trenton.
lines.research_note: All-stops roster. Tier B. Catenary. Trenton coordinate-shared with RL-002/091 — registry-bound.

lines.name: SEPTA Wilmington/Newark Line
lines.note: Down the Delaware shore of refineries and boroughs: Chester, Marcus Hook, over the state line to Wilmington and the college end at Newark.
lines.research_note: All-stops roster. Tier B. Catenary. Wilmington coordinate-shared with RL-002 — registry-bound.

lines.name: SEPTA Lansdale/Doylestown Line
lines.note: North through the old North Penn towns: Jenkintown, Ambler of the asbestos ghosts, Lansdale, and the county seat at Doylestown.
lines.research_note: All-stops roster (center-city through-running collapsed to 30th Street origin, declared). Tier B. Catenary.

lines.name: SEPTA West Trenton Line
lines.note: The Reading's route to the river: Jenkintown, the Pennypack crossings, Langhorne and Yardley, ending across the Delaware at West Trenton.
lines.research_note: All-stops roster. Tier B. Catenary.

lines.name: SEPTA Media/Wawa Line
lines.note: The trolley-suburb line grown up: Swarthmore's campus stop, Media's courthouse main street, and the 2022 extension to Wawa.
lines.research_note: All-stops roster. Tier B. Catenary.

lines.name: SEPTA Norristown/Manayunk Line
lines.note: Up the Schuylkill's east bank: Manayunk's mill walls at track level, Conshohocken, and Norristown's three-station finish.
lines.research_note: All-stops roster. Tier B. Catenary. Riverbank alignment — flood interest.

lines.name: SEPTA Warminster Line
lines.note: Off the main at Glenside: Ardsley, Willow Grove of the old amusement park, Hatboro, Warminster.
lines.research_note: All-stops roster. Tier B. Catenary. Trunk shared with RL-156/157 — registry-bound.

lines.name: SEPTA Cynwyd Line
lines.note: Three stops over the Schuylkill into Lower Merion — the smallest artery on the whole continent's sweep, and still a two-municipality line.
lines.research_note: All-stops roster. Tier B. Catenary.

lines.name: Metra UP North Line (Chicago–Kenosha)
lines.note: The North Shore in full: Evanston, the Winnetka cut, Ravinia's festival platform, the Lake Forests, Waukegan, and over the state line to Kenosha.
lines.research_note: All-stops roster (principal union). Tier B. Diesel.

lines.name: Metra UP Northwest Line (Chicago–Harvard)
lines.note: The northwest diagonal: Arlington Heights, Palatine, Barrington's horse country, Crystal Lake, and dairy-town Harvard at the end.
lines.research_note: All-stops roster (principal union). Tier B. Diesel.

lines.name: Metra UP West Line (Chicago–Elburn)
lines.note: Straight west on the Overland Route's first miles: Oak Park, Elmhurst, Wheaton, Geneva on the Fox, and the farm-edge terminus at Elburn.
lines.research_note: All-stops roster (principal union). Tier B. Diesel.

lines.name: Metra BNSF Line (Chicago–Aurora)
lines.note: The racetrack triple-main: Berwyn, the Grove towns, Naperville shared with the Zephyr itself, Route 59's park-and-ride sea, Aurora.
lines.research_note: All-stops roster (principal union). Tier B. Diesel. Naperville coordinate-shared with RL-011 — registry-bound.

lines.name: Metra Milwaukee District North Line (Chicago–Fox Lake)
lines.note: Glenview on the Builder's own rails, Libertyville, the chain-of-lakes country to Fox Lake.
lines.research_note: All-stops roster (principal union). Tier B. Diesel. Glenview coordinate-shared with RL-010/045 — registry-bound.

lines.name: Metra Milwaukee District West Line (Chicago–Elgin)
lines.note: Franklin Park's crossings, the Itasca–Bartlett string, and the Fox River mills at Elgin.
lines.research_note: All-stops roster (principal union). Tier B. Diesel.

lines.name: Metra Rock Island Line (Chicago–Joliet)
lines.note: The Rock's survivor: Blue Island, Tinley Park, New Lenox, into Joliet's union station beside the Eagle's own platform.
lines.research_note: All-stops roster (principal union; Beverly branch folded, declared). Tier B. Diesel. Joliet coordinate-shared with RL-030 — registry-bound.
