lines.name: Metra Electric Line (Chicago–University Park)
lines.note: The IC's electric main: Millennium under the park, Hyde Park's platforms, Kensington where the South Shore diverges, Homewood beside the City of New Orleans, University Park.
lines.research_note: All-stops roster (principal union; branch services folded, declared). Tier B. Catenary. Homewood coordinate-shared with RL-008 — registry-bound.

lines.name: Metra SouthWest Service (Chicago–Manhattan)
lines.note: The southwest diagonal: Oak Lawn, Palos Heights, Orland Park's park-and-rides, and the prairie edge at Manhattan.
lines.research_note: All-stops roster (principal union). Tier B. Diesel.

lines.name: Metra North Central Service (Chicago–Antioch)
lines.note: The O'Hare corridor and the lake towns: Wheeling, Buffalo Grove, Mundelein, to Antioch at the Wisconsin line.
lines.research_note: All-stops roster (principal union). Tier B. Diesel.

lines.name: Metra Heritage Corridor (Chicago–Joliet)
lines.note: Three trains a day on the oldest corridor out of town: the canal route through Summit and Lemont to Joliet.
lines.research_note: All-stops roster. Tier B. Diesel. Joliet registry-bound.

lines.name: GO Lakeshore West Line (Toronto–Niagara Falls)
lines.note: The lake's western rim: Port Credit, Oakville, Burlington under the escarpment, Hamilton, and the seasonal reach through wine country to the falls.
lines.research_note: All-stops roster (Niagara extension in union). Tier B. Diesel. Oakville/Aldershot/St. Catharines/Niagara shared with RL-064/069 — registry-bound.

lines.name: GO Lakeshore East Line (Toronto–Oshawa)
lines.note: East along the bluffs: Scarborough, Guildwood over the lake, Pickering and Ajax, Whitby, Oshawa of the auto plants.
lines.research_note: All-stops roster. Tier B. Diesel. Oshawa shared with RL-068 — registry-bound.

lines.name: GO Milton Line
lines.note: The CP corridor west: Cooksville, Streetsville on the Credit, Meadowvale, and Milton at the escarpment's foot.
lines.research_note: All-stops roster. Tier B. Diesel.

lines.name: GO Kitchener Line
lines.note: Northwest through Brampton and Georgetown, Guelph's limestone downtown, and the twin cities at Kitchener.
lines.research_note: All-stops roster. Tier B. Diesel.

lines.name: GO Barrie Line
lines.note: Straight north out of the city: Maple and King City, Aurora and Newmarket, the Holland Marsh at Bradford, and Barrie on Kempenfelt Bay.
lines.research_note: All-stops roster. Tier B. Diesel.

lines.name: GO Richmond Hill Line
lines.note: Up the Don Valley's floor to Richmond Hill — the short line with the flood-prone miles.
lines.research_note: All-stops roster. Tier B. Diesel. Don Valley alignment — flood interest.

lines.name: GO Stouffville Line
lines.note: The Markham corridor: Unionville's old village, Markham, Mount Joy, and Stouffville at the moraine.
lines.research_note: All-stops roster. Tier B. Diesel.

lines.name: West Coast Express (Vancouver–Mission)
lines.note: Up the Fraser on the CP main: Port Moody's inlet, the Tri-Cities, Pitt Meadows' dike country, and Mission City an hour upriver.
lines.research_note: All-stops roster. Tier B. Diesel. Fraser lowland alignment — SLR interest. Waterfront ~2 km from Pacific Central; distinct terminals, no junction claimed.

lines.name: exo Vaudreuil–Hudson Line
lines.note: The lakeshore west: Dorval, Pointe-Claire, Sainte-Anne at the island's tip, and over the water to Vaudreuil and Hudson.
lines.research_note: All-stops roster (principal union). Tier B. Diesel.

lines.name: exo Saint-Jérôme Line
lines.note: North through Laval to the Laurentian gateway at Saint-Jérôme.
lines.research_note: All-stops roster (principal union). Tier B. Diesel.

lines.name: exo Mont-Saint-Hilaire Line
lines.note: The south shore east: Saint-Hubert, the Richelieu crossing, and the mountain village terminus.
lines.research_note: All-stops roster (principal union). Tier B. Diesel. Montréal Central/Saint-Lambert shared with RL-063/068/071 — registry-bound.

lines.name: exo Candiac Line
lines.note: Over the Saint-Laurent to the south-shore towns: Sainte-Catherine by the seaway locks, Delson, Candiac.
lines.research_note: All-stops roster (principal union). Tier B. Diesel.

lines.name: REM (Brossard–Deux-Montagnes)
lines.note: The automated light line that replaced a railway and outgrew a metro: over the new Champlain span from Brossard, under Mount Royal, and out to Deux-Montagnes every few minutes, no driver aboard.
lines.research_note: Principal-station roster (airport/Anse-à-l'Orme branches deferred). Tier B. Catenary (1.5 kV light metro). ADMITTED under D2v2's connects-test — crosses the river and reaches 30 km out despite metro technology; judgment flagged for Laurie's review. Gare Centrale shared with RL-068/071/183 — registry-bound.

lines.name: Tren Maya (Palenque–Cancún–Tulum)
lines.note: The peninsula circled by rail for the first time: out of the Chiapas forest at Palenque, Campeche's walls, Mérida, the cenote country past Valladolid, Cancún, and down the Caribbean shore to Tulum.
lines.research_note: Principal-station roster (full loop's southern arc deferred). Tier B. Diesel. Opened 2023–24 — the newest major railway in the hemisphere. X010 derivation interest maximal: the peninsula is inundation-coded lowland.
lines.note_2060: The youngest railway in the layer, grayed whole: {DROWNED_STOPS} — built on the lowest land in the hemisphere's rail map, and the first to give it back. Kept on the books entire.

lines.name: El Chepe (Los Mochis–Chihuahua)
lines.note: The impossible railroad: up from the Pacific plain into the Copper Canyon's walls — 37 bridges, 86 tunnels — Divisadero on the very rim, Creel in the Tarahumara pines, and out onto the high plain to Chihuahua.
lines.research_note: Full passenger-station roster. Tier B. Diesel. Canyon shaping hand-placed. Bridge/tunnel counts are engineering constants, retained in copy.
lines.note_2060: The canyon keeps its train: the line lives from {NEW_TERMINI} across the high plain, and below the rim the coastal descent — {DROWNED_STOPS} — grays toward the sea it used to reach.

lines.name: El Insurgente (Zinacantepec–Observatorio)
lines.note: Toluca to the capital under wire: out of the Nevado's shadow, the Lerma flats, the long tunnel under the Sierra de las Cruces, and Santa Fe's towers to Observatorio.
lines.research_note: Full station roster. Tier B. Catenary. Two-state artery, D2v2 exemplar.

lines.name: Tren Suburbano (Buenavista–Cuautitlán)
lines.note: The valley's electric workhorse: Buenavista's old terminal reborn, Tlalnepantla, Lechería, Cuautitlán — a million riders' worth of Estado de México on one line.
lines.research_note: Full station roster. Tier B. Catenary.

lines.name: Tren Interoceánico Línea Z (Salina Cruz–Coatzacoalcos)
lines.note: Ocean to ocean across the isthmus: the Pacific at Salina Cruz, Tehuantepec, the windy gap at Ixtepec, and down the Gulf slope to Coatzacoalcos — Mexico's answer to the canal, running again since 2023.
lines.research_note: Principal-station roster (Línea FA to Palenque deferred). Tier B. Diesel. Both termini are coastal-lowland ports — X010 derivation interest.
lines.note_2060: One ocean left: the line runs from the Gulf slope to {NEW_TERMINI}, and the Pacific gate at {DROWNED_STOPS} waits grayed — the isthmus crossing half-kept, like the trade it carried.

lines.name: Mumbai Suburban Western Line (Churchgate–Virar)
lines.note: The lifeline down the western shore: Churchgate's Art Deco terminus, Mumbai Central, Bandra, Andheri, Borivali, and the tidal-flat run out to Virar — seven million journeys a day on one quadruple-track spine.
lines.research_note: Principal + major-halt roster (the line has ~30 stations; principal union given). Tier B. Catenary. Runs in canon: Mumbai is a defended heat-refuge (R092 Konkan recovered strip), not a drowned delta — the graying I expected is not there.

lines.name: Mumbai Suburban Central Line (CSMT–Kalyan)
lines.note: The original of 1853 — the first railway in Asia — out of Victoria Terminus through Byculla and Dadar, Kurla's junction, Ghatkopar, Thane across the creek, and up to Kalyan where the lines for the whole subcontinent divide.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Catenary. 'First railway in Asia' — historical constant, in copy. Runs (defended-refuge finding).

lines.name: Mumbai Suburban Harbour Line (CSMT–Panvel)
lines.note: Across the harbour reclamations: the docks at Sewri, Vashi over the creek bridge into the planned grid of Navi Mumbai, and out to Panvel at the mainland's edge.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Catenary. Vashi creek and Navi Mumbai reclamations noted — the SLR overlay's Mumbai story rides here even though the zone map keeps the city.

lines.name: RER A (Saint-Germain / Cergy / Poissy — Boissy / Marne-la-Vallée)
lines.note: The western towns — Saint-Germain-en-Laye, Cergy — through the central tunnel under Étoile and Châtelet, and out east to Vincennes, Marne-la-Vallée and the gates of Disneyland: a line that moves a small country's worth of people daily.
lines.research_note: Busiest line in the Western world outside Asia (2026, expiry-tagged). Principal + branch-junction roster. Tier C (partial). Catenary. Runs (no European X zones, B18).

lines.name: RER B (Aéroport CDG / Mitry — Robinson / Saint-Rémy)
lines.note: The airport-and-university axis: Charles de Gaulle in the northern plain, the Gare du Nord, the central spine to Denfert, and the southern reach past Bourg-la-Reine to the Saclay science towns at Saint-Rémy-lès-Chevreuse.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Catenary.

lines.name: RER C (the Seine-valley line)
lines.note: The river's own RER: Versailles-Château at one end, along the Left Bank quais past the Musée d'Orsay's platform, Austerlitz, and out southeast down the Seine toward Massy and Dourdan — the longest, most many-branched line of the network.
lines.research_note: Principal + trunk roster (RER C is famously branchy; principal stations given, roster partial). Tier B. Catenary. Seine-quai alignment — the Paris flood story (2016/1910 precedent) rides here for the SLR/river overlay.

lines.name: RER D (Orry-la-Ville — Melun / Malesherbes)
lines.note: North-to-southeast across the whole région: the Oise towns, through the Châtelet core shared with the others, and down the Seine-et-Marne to Melun and the Gâtinais at Malesherbes.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Catenary.

lines.name: RER E (Haussmann / Nanterre — Chelles / Tournan)
lines.note: The newest line, driven west under the city: Haussmann-Saint-Lazare and the new Nanterre–La Défense tunnel at one end, the Marne valley towns — Chelles, Tournan — at the other.
lines.research_note: Principal roster (2024 western extension included). Tier B. Catenary.

lines.name: S-Bahn Rhein-Ruhr S1 (Dortmund–Solingen)
lines.note: Across the Ruhr conurbation coast to coast of cities: Dortmund, Bochum, Essen, Mülheim, Duisburg, Düsseldorf, and up the Wupper to Solingen — a dozen cities on one S-Bahn, the definition of a polycentric region.
lines.research_note: Principal roster (the S1 stops at ~40; principal cities given). Tier B. Catenary. The purest D2v2 case on Earth: no single 'home city.' Runs (no European X zones).

lines.name: S-Bahn Berlin S1 (Oranienburg–Wannsee)
lines.note: North to southwest across the capital: Oranienburg in Brandenburg, Frohnau's garden suburb, the Nordbahn into the centre at Friedrichstraße, and out through Steglitz to the lake bourg at Wannsee.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Catenary (750 V third rail — Berlin S-Bahn technology, declared). Two-Land artery (Brandenburg/Berlin).

lines.name: S-Bahn München S1 (Freising/Flughafen–Ostbahnhof)
lines.note: The Bavarian trunk through the Stammstrecke: the airport and Freising in the northern plain, Moosach, the central tunnel under Marienplatz, and out to the Ostbahnhof — every S-line sharing the same two-track core.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Catenary. Munich Stammstrecke shared-core noted.

lines.name: S-Bahn Hamburg S1 (Wedel–Poppenbüttel)
lines.note: Across the Elbe-mouth city: Wedel down the river's north bank, Altona, the Jungfernstieg tunnel under the Binnenalster, the airport branch, and out to Poppenbüttel on the Alster's upper reach.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Catenary (1200 V third rail — Hamburg technology). Hamburg is a defended heat-refuge (C180) — runs, defended.

lines.name: JR Keihin-Tōhoku Line (Ōmiya–Yokohama–Ōfuna)
lines.note: The megalopolis spine at its densest: Saitama's capital at Ōmiya, down through Akabane and the eastern loop stations, Tokyo and Shinagawa, over the border to Kawasaki and Yokohama, and out to Ōfuna — three prefectures' cities strung on one line, a train every two minutes.
lines.research_note: Principal-station roster (line has 46 stations). Tier C (partial). Catenary. Runs: Kanto is R096 (recovered), Tokyo a defended heat-refuge. Ōmiya/Yokohama distinct municipalities — D2v2 satisfied by geography (no own C-IDs in roster).

lines.name: JR Tōkaidō Line (Tokyo–Odawara–Atami)
lines.note: The old road's rails: Tokyo out through Yokohama, the Shōnan coast at Ōfuna and Fujisawa, Hiratsuka and Odawara under the mountains, and the hot-spring gateway at Atami.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier C (partial). Catenary. Odawara junction with the Shinkansen (RL-001) — registry-bound.

lines.name: JR Yokosuka Line (Tokyo–Kurihama)
lines.note: Down the Miura peninsula: Tokyo underground to Shinagawa, the temple city at Kamakura, the naval port at Yokosuka, and the peninsula's end at Kurihama.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier C (partial). Catenary. Tokyo–Ōfuna trunk shared with RL-203/204 — registry-bound.

lines.name: JR Chūō Line (Tokyo–Takao)
lines.note: Due west into the Tama cities: Shinjuku, the student towns, Kokubunji, the transport hub at Tachikawa, Hachiōji's own city, and the mountain gateway at Takao.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier C (partial). Catenary. Distinct Tama-region cities (Tachikawa/Hachiōji).

lines.name: JR Sōbu Line (Tokyo–Chiba)
lines.note: East across the lowland to the bay capital: Kinshichō, over the Edo and Ara rivers, Ichikawa and Funabashi's suburbs, and into Chiba.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier C (partial). Catenary. Bay-lowland alignment — the SLR overlay's Tokyo story rides here though R096 keeps the zone.

lines.name: JR Takasaki/Utsunomiya Line (Ueno–Ōmiya–north)
lines.note: The northern trunk out of Ueno: Akabane, Ōmiya where the lines fork, Kumagaya toward Takasaki and Utsunomiya toward the mountains — the Kanto plain's grain corridor at commuter frequency near town.
lines.research_note: Principal roster (Takasaki branch given; Utsunomiya fork noted). Tier C (partial). Catenary.

lines.name: JR Jōban Line (Ueno–Toride)
lines.note: Northeast to the Chiba–Ibaraki edge: Nippori, Matsudo over the Edogawa, Kashiwa, and Toride at the Tone river — the near reach of the long line to Mito.
lines.research_note: Principal roster (commuter section to Toride; Mito extension deferred). Tier C (partial). Catenary.

lines.name: Tōkyū Tōyoko Line (Shibuya–Yokohama)
lines.note: The classic interurban: Shibuya underground, the desirable Den-en-chōfu suburbs, Musashi-Kosugi's towers, and into Yokohama — the private railway that built its own commuter belt between two cities.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier C (partial). Catenary. Yokohama shared with JR lines — registry-bound.

lines.name: Odakyū Odawara Line (Shinjuku–Odawara)
lines.note: Out to the mountains and the sea: Shinjuku, the dense inner suburbs at Shimo-Kitazawa, Machida's own city, the Tama hills, and Odawara for the Hakone transfer — the Romancecar's road.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier C (partial). Catenary. Odawara shared — registry-bound.

lines.name: Keiō Line (Shinjuku–Keiō-Hachiōji)
lines.note: The Kōshū road's rail: Shinjuku, Meidaimae's junction, Chōfu, the Tama New Town feeders, and Hachiōji by the western hills.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier C (partial). Catenary. Hachiōji distinct city (also on RL-206) — registry-bound.

lines.name: Keikyū Main Line (Shinagawa–Yokohama–Uraga)
lines.note: The bayshore private line: Shinagawa, over the border to Kawasaki, Yokohama, and down the Miura coast through Yokosuka to Uraga where Perry's ships once anchored — plus the airport branch at Haneda.
lines.research_note: Principal roster (Haneda branch noted). Tier C (partial). Catenary. Kawasaki/Yokohama shared — registry-bound.

lines.name: Keisei Main Line (Ueno–Narita)
lines.note: To the airport the old way: Keisei-Ueno, Nippori, the Edogawa crossing into Chiba, Funabashi, Narita's temple town, and the airport under the plain.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier C (partial). Catenary. Narita/Funabashi distinct Chiba cities.

lines.name: Tōbu Tōjō Line (Ikebukuro–Kawagoe–Ogawamachi)
lines.note: Northwest into Saitama: Ikebukuro, the Nerima suburbs, the little-Edo city at Kawagoe, and out to the Ogawamachi hills.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier C (partial). Catenary. Kawagoe distinct Saitama city.

lines.name: Seibu Ikebukuro Line (Ikebukuro–Hannō)
lines.note: The other Ikebukuro line: the Nerima corridor, Tokorozawa's junction city, Hibarigaoka, and out to Hannō at the Chichibu foothills.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier C (partial). Catenary. Tokorozawa distinct Saitama city.

lines.name: South Western Main Line (Waterloo–Southampton/Portsmouth)
lines.opened: Victorian main lines, modern franchised operation
lines.note: Out of Waterloo across the Surrey commuter belt — Woking, Guildford's junction — the Hampshire heaths at Farnborough and Basingstoke, and down to Winchester and the water at Southampton, with the Portsmouth Direct branching at Woking.
lines.research_note: Principal roster (all-stops union of the fast lines). Tier B. Third-rail (declared catenary-class). London Waterloo → C151.

lines.name: Brighton Main Line (London–Gatwick–Brighton)
lines.opened: Victorian main lines, modern franchised operation
lines.note: The classic commuter race to the sea: Victoria and London Bridge, the East Croydon junction, Gatwick's airport platforms, the Weald at Haywards Heath, and down the Clayton tunnel into Brighton.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Third-rail. London Victoria → C151.

lines.name: Thameslink (Bedford–London–Gatwick–Brighton)
lines.opened: Victorian main lines, modern franchised operation
lines.note: The cross-London spine: Bedford and Luton down through the core tunnel under the city — St Pancras, Farringdon, London Bridge — and out the other side to Gatwick and Brighton, north and south counties sewn through the middle.
lines.research_note: Principal roster (core + principal termini; the Wimbledon/Sutton loops deferred). Tier B. Mixed (dual-voltage). St Pancras → C151.

lines.name: Chatham & Dover (Southeastern, London–Dover via the Medway)
lines.opened: Victorian main lines, modern franchised operation
lines.note: Down through the north-Kent estuary towns: the Medway at Rochester and Chatham, Faversham of the creek, Canterbury's cathedral, and the Channel at Dover — the marsh country close on the left the whole way.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Third-rail. THE Thames-estuary line: the Medway-side stations touch the Thames defended ring — flagged for the cartographer's UK coastal audit. London Victoria → C151.

lines.name: c2c (Fenchurch Street–Basildon–Southend)
lines.opened: Victorian main lines, modern franchised operation
lines.note: The Essex Thames-side: out of the City at Fenchurch Street along the north bank — Barking, the Tilbury docks, the marshes at Benfleet — to Leigh-on-Sea's cockle sheds and Southend on its pier.
lines.research_note: Principal roster (both the Tilbury loop and the Laindon inland route folded to the principal union). Tier B. Third-rail. THE most Thames-exposed UK line: nearly its whole length is in the estuary defended ring — flagged for UK coastal audit.

lines.name: Great Eastern Main Line (Greater Anglia, London–Norwich)
lines.opened: Victorian main lines, modern franchised operation
lines.note: Up through East Anglia: Stratford, the Essex towns at Chelmsford and Colchester, the Suffolk border at Manningtree, Ipswich, and the long straight to Norwich across the flat land.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Catenary. Liverpool Street → C151.

lines.name: West Anglia Main Line (London–Cambridge)
lines.opened: Victorian main lines, modern franchised operation
lines.note: North up the Lea valley: Tottenham Hale, the Hertfordshire towns at Broxbourne and Bishop's Stortford, Stansted's airport branch, and the university city at Cambridge.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Catenary. Liverpool Street → C151.

lines.name: Chiltern Main Line (Marylebone–Birmingham)
lines.opened: Victorian main lines, modern franchised operation
lines.note: The other way to Birmingham: Marylebone out through the Chilterns — High Wycombe, Princes Risborough — Bicester, Banbury of the cross, Leamington Spa, and into Birmingham Moor Street.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Diesel. London Marylebone → C151; Birmingham → (no roster C-ID).

lines.name: Elizabeth line (Reading–London–Shenfield/Abbey Wood)
lines.opened: Victorian main lines, modern franchised operation
lines.note: The 2022 cross-London railway: Reading and Heathrow in the west, the deep new core under the centre — Paddington, Farringdon, Liverpool Street, Canary Wharf — and out east to Shenfield and, under the river, Abbey Wood.
lines.research_note: Principal roster (both eastern branches folded to principal union). Tier B. Catenary. Connects Reading/Slough/Shenfield to London — D2v2 by geography. Paddington → C151.

lines.name: Merseyrail (Liverpool–Southport / Chester / Ormskirk)
lines.opened: Victorian main lines, modern franchised operation
lines.note: The network under the Mersey: the Wirral lines through the Birkenhead tunnel to Chester and West Kirby, and the Northern lines up the coast to Southport and inland to Ormskirk and Kirkby — Merseyside's own metro-scale main railway.
lines.research_note: Principal roster (Wirral + Northern principal union). Tier B. Third-rail. Distinct Merseyside towns — D2v2 like the LIRR. Liverpool → (no roster C-ID).

lines.name: Glasgow North Clyde & Argyle Lines (Helensburgh/Balloch–Airdrie)
lines.opened: Victorian main lines, modern franchised operation
lines.note: Scotland's great suburban network: the Clyde coast at Helensburgh and Balloch on Loch Lomond, through the Glasgow core tunnels — Partick, the low-level platforms — and out east through Bellgrove to Airdrie and on toward Edinburgh.
lines.research_note: Busiest railway outside London (2026, expiry-tagged). Principal roster (North Clyde + Argyle union). Tier B. Catenary. Glasgow → C149.

lines.name: Northern (Manchester–Leeds via the Calder Valley)
lines.opened: Victorian main lines, modern franchised operation
lines.note: Across the Pennine mill country: Manchester Victoria, Rochdale, the Calder Valley towns — Hebden Bridge, Halifax — and Bradford and Leeds beyond the watershed.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Diesel. Manchester → C152; Leeds → C150.

lines.name: Indian Pacific (Sydney–Perth)
lines.opened: 1970 — the transcontinental, the world’s longest straight track
lines.note: Three nights across the continent: out of Sydney over the Blue Mountains, the silver city at Broken Hill, Adelaide on its gulf, then the Nullarbor — 478 km without a curve, no town for a day — Kalgoorlie's goldfields, and down to the Indian Ocean at Perth.
lines.research_note: Principal-station roster. Tier B. Diesel. The Nullarbor is X012 (wet-bulb, DRY) — expect x-transit: maintained crossing, Perth live on the far side. 'Longest straight' — geographic constant, research.

lines.name: The Overland (Melbourne–Adelaide)
lines.note: The daylight run across the border: out of Southern Cross through the Wimmera wheat country, Ararat and Horsham, the Coorong's edge, and into Adelaide — one of the oldest named trains still running.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Diesel. All R125/R129 (recovered) — runs whole.

lines.name: NSW XPT (Sydney–Melbourne)
lines.opened: XPT service 1982 on the Main South
lines.note: The inland route between the two great cities: Sydney out through the Southern Highlands, Goulburn, the national capital's junction at Yass, Albury on the Murray, and down through Victoria's north to Melbourne.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Diesel. Runs whole (R125).

lines.name: NSW XPT (Sydney–Brisbane, North Coast)
lines.note: Up the coast north: the Hawkesbury crossing, the Hunter at Maitland, the Manning and Macleay river towns, Coffs Harbour by the sea, Grafton on the Clarence, and over the border to Brisbane.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Diesel. Runs whole (R125). Brisbane → C471.

lines.name: Spirit of Queensland (Brisbane–Cairns)
lines.opened: North Coast line to the tropics; tilt-train service
lines.note: The long tropical coast: Brisbane up through the Sunshine Coast hinterland, Bundaberg's cane country, Rockhampton on the Tropic of Capricorn, the Whitsundays at Proserpine, Townsville, and Cairns under the wet-tropics ranges.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Diesel. Derivation interest: Cairns and the far north are X012 (wet-bulb) — expect the tropical end to gray. Brisbane → C471.

lines.name: Sydney Central Coast & Newcastle Line
lines.note: The commute the Hawkesbury shaped: out of Central under the harbour bridge approaches, the river crossing at Hawkesbury, the Central Coast towns at Gosford and Wyong, and up to Newcastle where the line meets the sea.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Catenary to Gosford, diesel north — mixed. Newcastle → C476.

lines.name: Sydney South Coast Line (Sydney–Wollongong–Kiama)
lines.note: Over the escarpment and down to the sea: the Illawarra line through the Royal National Park, the dramatic Stanwell Park descent, the steel city at Wollongong, and the coast towns to Kiama's blowhole.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Catenary to Kiama. Wollongong (no roster C-ID).

lines.name: Sydney Blue Mountains Line (Sydney–Lithgow)
lines.opened: Main Western line over the mountains
lines.note: Up the great western climb: Parramatta, Penrith on the Nepean, then the mountain villages one above the next — Springwood, Katoomba by the Three Sisters, Mount Victoria — and the Zig Zag descent to Lithgow.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Catenary to Lithgow.

lines.name: V/Line Geelong & Warrnambool
lines.note: West around the bay and along the coast: Geelong past the You Yangs, Colac's lake country, and the dairy towns to the Shipwreck Coast port at Warrnambool.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Diesel (Geelong electrified-adjacent; mixed). Geelong (no C-ID).

lines.name: V/Line Gippsland (Melbourne–Bairnsdale)
lines.note: East through the Latrobe Valley: Dandenong, the power-station towns at Moe and Morwell, Traralgon, and the lakes country to Bairnsdale.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Diesel.

lines.name: V/Line Bendigo & Swan Hill
lines.note: North to the goldfields and beyond: Sunbury, the spa at Kyneton, Castlemaine's heritage, Bendigo's poppet heads, and on across the Mallee to Swan Hill on the Murray.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Diesel/electric mixed.

lines.name: V/Line Ballarat & Ararat
lines.note: West to the other goldfield: Melton, Bacchus Marsh in its valley, Ballarat of Eureka, and on to Ararat under the Grampians' edge.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Electric/diesel mixed. Ararat junction with RL-230 — registry-bound.

lines.name: Queensland Rail Gold Coast Line (Brisbane–Varsity Lakes)
lines.note: South to the beaches: Brisbane out through the southern suburbs, Beenleigh, the Logan crossing, and down the Gold Coast to Robina and Varsity Lakes.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Catenary. NOTE: Gold Coast is roster-status contracting/excluded (C474) though its zone is R125 (recovered) — zone governs derivation (runs), tension flagged for research. Brisbane → C471.

lines.name: Queensland Rail Sunshine Coast Line (Brisbane–Gympie North)
lines.note: North to the other coast: Caboolture, the Glass House Mountains' volcanic plugs, Nambour in the cane, and up to Gympie North.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Catenary to Nambour, diesel north — mixed. Trunk shared with RL-233 — registry-bound.

lines.name: Transperth Mandurah Line (Perth–Mandurah)
lines.note: Down the freeway median at speed: Perth south through Cockburn and Rockingham's junction, and out to the estuary city at Mandurah — a distinct city, a new-build line built for it.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Catenary. Perth → C483.

lines.name: Adelaide Metro Gawler & Seaford Lines
lines.note: The two arms of Adelaide's electrified network: north up the plains through Salisbury to the old copper town at Gawler, and south down the coast through Noarlunga to Seaford.
lines.research_note: Principal roster (Gawler + Seaford union). Tier B. Catenary. Adelaide → C470.

lines.name: Línea Roca (Constitución–La Plata)
lines.note: South along the estuary to the provincial capital: out of Constitución through Avellaneda and the industrial suburbs, Quilmes and Berazategui on the low coast, and into La Plata of the perfect grid.
lines.research_note: Principal roster (La Plata branch; the Ezeiza/Bosques/Korn branches folded to union). Tier B. Catenary. La Plata sits in the Río de la Plata ring (unvalidated → cartographer queue); the SA coast is unaudited so it runs, flagged. Constitución = central BA terminus.

lines.name: Línea Sarmiento (Once–Moreno–Mercedes)
lines.note: Due west across the pampa's edge: Once de Septiembre in the centre, Caballito and Flores, Morón and Castelar in the near suburbs, Moreno, and on to Mercedes in the wheat country.
lines.research_note: Principal roster (Moreno electric + Mercedes diesel extension). Tier B. Catenary/mixed. Runs west, away from the estuary — no coastal exposure. Once = central BA.

lines.name: Línea Mitre (Retiro–Tigre)
lines.note: North to the delta: out of Retiro along the river bluff, Belgrano and Núñez, the riverside suburbs at Olivos and San Isidro, and Tigre where the Paraná breaks into a thousand channels.
lines.research_note: Principal roster (Tigre branch; Bartolomé Mitre/Suárez branches folded). Tier B. Catenary. Tigre is delta-edge — the coarse Plata box misses it (stops at -34.7); noted for cartographer refinement. Retiro = central BA.

lines.name: Línea San Martín (Retiro–Pilar–Cabred)
lines.note: Northwest through the growing suburbs: Palermo and Chacarita, Devoto, Hurlingham and San Miguel, and out to Pilar's country estates and Cabred.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Diesel (electrification long-promised). Retiro = central BA.

lines.name: Línea Belgrano Norte (Retiro–Villa Rosa)
lines.note: The narrow-gauge north line: Retiro's Belgrano terminus, through Vicente López and Boulogne, to the far suburbs at Villa Rosa.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Diesel (metre gauge). Retiro = central BA.

lines.name: Línea Belgrano Sur (Buenos Aires–González Catán)
lines.note: The southwestern narrow gauge: from the Sáenz/Buenos Aires terminus out through Tapiales and the working suburbs to González Catán in La Matanza.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Diesel (metre gauge).

lines.name: Línea Urquiza (Lacroze–General Lemos)
lines.note: The northwest narrow-gauge line: Federico Lacroze by the Chacarita cemetery, through Caseros and the near suburbs, to General Lemos.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Catenary (metre gauge, electrified).

lines.name: CPTM Linha 7-Rubi (Luz–Jundiaí)
lines.note: Northwest up the old Santos-Jundiaí railway: Luz's great station, the industrial suburbs at Barra Funda and Perus, Caieiras, and Jundiaí at the range's foot.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Catenary. Luz → C093 (São Paulo). Connects distinct cities (Jundiaí, Caieiras) — D2v2.

lines.name: CPTM Linha 11-Coral (Luz–Estudantes/Guaianases)
lines.note: East across the sprawl: Luz and Brás, the vast eastern zone at Itaquera and Guaianases, and out to Mogi das Cruzes' student town.
lines.research_note: Principal roster (the express-east line). Tier B. Catenary. Reaches distinct cities (Mogi das Cruzes) — D2v2. Brás/Luz = central SP.

lines.name: CPTM Linha 9-Esmeralda (Osasco–Grajaú)
lines.note: Down the Pinheiros river: Osasco's junction, the corporate towers at Vila Olímpia and Berrini along the water, and south to Grajaú.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Catenary. Pinheiros-riverside alignment — SP's flood story, though R025 keeps the zone. Osasco/Grajaú distinct municipalities.

lines.name: Tren Nos / Rancagua (Santiago–Rancagua)
lines.note: South down the Central Valley: out of the Alameda terminus through San Bernardo, the vineyard towns at Buin and Paine, and into Rancagua of the copper country.
lines.research_note: Principal roster (Metrotren Nos + Rancagua express). Tier B. Catenary. Santiago → C123. Rancagua a distinct city — D2v2.

lines.name: AVE Madrid–Valencia
lines.note: East to the Mediterranean in ninety minutes: Madrid Atocha across the Meseta, Cuenca's high new station, the Requena pass, and down to Valencia by the sea.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Catenary. Runs whole (R042 recovered — the hot Meseta is kept). Madrid → C169; Valencia → C175.

lines.name: AVE Madrid–Córdoba–Málaga
lines.note: South over the Despeñaperros to the Costa del Sol: Ciudad Real, Córdoba of the Mezquita, Puente Genil, and Málaga by the sea.
lines.research_note: Principal roster (shares Madrid–Córdoba trunk with RL-111 Sevilla). Tier B. Catenary. Málaga → C171.

lines.name: AVE Madrid–Galicia (Ourense–Santiago–Vigo)
lines.note: Northwest to the Atlantic corner: over the Guadarrama, Zamora on the Duero, the new Ourense gateway, Santiago de Compostela of the pilgrims, and Vigo on its ría.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Catenary. Santiago → C229; Vigo → C230.

lines.name: Mediterranean Corridor (Barcelona–Valencia–Alicante)
lines.note: Down the whole eastern coast: Barcelona Sants, Tarragona's Camp, the Ebro delta flats, Castellón, Valencia, and on through the huerta to Alicante.
lines.research_note: Principal roster (AVE/Euromed spine). Tier B. Catenary. THE Ebro-delta line: the Tarragona–Castellón flats sit in the Ebro submergence ring (unvalidated → cartographer queue; Iberia is unaudited so it runs, flagged). Barcelona → C165; Valencia → C175.

lines.name: Alvia Madrid–Bilbao
lines.note: North to the Basque coast: Madrid up through Burgos of the cathedral, Miranda de Ebro, Vitoria's plain, and down to Bilbao on its estuary.
lines.research_note: Principal roster (pre-Basque-Y alignment). Tier B. Catenary. Bilbao → C166.

lines.name: Cercanías Madrid C-2/C-7 (Guadalajara–Madrid–Alcalá)
lines.note: The Henares corridor through the capital: Guadalajara and Alcalá de Henares of Cervantes, the great Atocha–Sol–Chamartín tunnel, and out to the northern suburbs.
lines.research_note: Principal roster (the busy Henares + core tunnel). Tier B. Catenary. Alcalá/Guadalajara distinct cities — D2v2. Madrid → C169.

lines.name: Cercanías Madrid C-3/C-4 (Aranjuez–Madrid–Colmenar/Alcobendas)
lines.note: North-south across Madrid: the royal town of Aranjuez, Getafe and the southern belt, through Atocha and Chamartín, and out to Colmenar Viejo in the sierra's foothills.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Catenary. Aranjuez/Colmenar distinct towns.

lines.name: Rodalies Barcelona R1 (Mataró–Barcelona–Molins)
lines.note: Along the Maresme shore and through the city: Mataró and the coast towns on the sand, Barcelona's Sants and Plaça Catalunya, and inland to Molins de Rei.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Catenary. The Maresme coast — SLR interest though R042 keeps the zone. Barcelona → C165.

lines.name: Rodalies Barcelona R4 (Manresa–Barcelona–Vilafranca)
lines.note: Across Catalonia through the city: Manresa under Montserrat, Terrassa and Sabadell's industrial cities, Barcelona, and the wine town of Vilafranca del Penedès.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Catenary. Terrassa/Sabadell/Manresa distinct cities — D2v2.

lines.name: Cercanías Valencia (Gandia–Valencia–Castellón)
lines.note: Up the coast and the huerta: Gandia in the orange country, Valencia's Nord station, Sagunt of the castle, and Castellón to the north.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Catenary. Coastal huerta — Ebro/Med SLR interest. Valencia → C175.

lines.name: Cercanías Bilbao (Bilbao–Santurtzi/Muskiz)
lines.note: Down the Nervión estuary to the sea: Bilbao Abando, the industrial ría past Barakaldo and Sestao, and out to Santurtzi and Muskiz at the river mouth.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Catenary. Nervión estuary — SLR interest. Bilbao → C166.

lines.name: Cercanías Sevilla (Lora del Río–Sevilla–Utrera)
lines.note: Along the Guadalquivir valley: the river towns at Lora del Río and Cantillana, Sevilla's Santa Justa, and south to Dos Hermanas and Utrera.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Catenary. Guadalquivir valley. Seville → C173.

lines.name: Cercanías Málaga (Málaga–Fuengirola)
lines.note: The Costa del Sol commuter line: Málaga and the airport, Torremolinos on the cliffs, Benalmádena, and Fuengirola down the beach coast.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Catenary. Costa del Sol beachfront — SLR interest. Málaga → C171.

lines.name: FEVE Cantabrian Coast (Bilbao–Santander–Oviedo)
lines.note: The metre-gauge along the green north coast: Bilbao out along the Basque shore, Santander on its bay, the Picos de Europa's foothills, and Oviedo of the Asturias.
lines.research_note: Principal roster (the old FEVE narrow gauge). Tier B. Diesel/electric (metre gauge). Cantabrian coast — SLR interest. Bilbao → C166.

lines.name: LGV Atlantique (Paris–Bordeaux)
lines.note: Southwest to the Garonne in two hours: Montparnasse out through the Beauce, Tours by the Loire, Poitiers, Angoulême, and Bordeaux of the wine.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Catenary. Runs whole (R037 recovered). Paris → C140; Bordeaux → C167.

lines.name: LGV Nord (Paris–Lille)
lines.note: North to Flanders and the Channel tunnel: Gare du Nord out across Picardy, the TGV junction at Roissy, and Lille where the lines for London and Brussels divide.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Catenary. Lille → C137; junctions with RL-120 Eurostar.

lines.name: LGV Est (Paris–Strasbourg)
lines.note: East to the Rhine: Gare de l'Est across Champagne, Reims of the cathedral, the Lorraine junction at Meuse, Nancy, and Strasbourg by the German border.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Catenary. Strasbourg (no roster C-ID).

lines.name: LGV Bretagne (Paris–Rennes–Nantes)
lines.note: West to the Atlantic provinces: Montparnasse to Le Mans, then the fork — Rennes for Brittany, Nantes for the Loire mouth.
lines.research_note: Principal roster (both western forks folded to principal union). Tier B. Catenary. Rennes → C141; Nantes → C139.

lines.name: TGV/Intercités Sud (Bordeaux–Toulouse–Marseille)
lines.note: The southern transversal: Bordeaux along the Garonne to Toulouse, Carcassonne of the walls, Narbonne, the Languedoc coast at Montpellier and Nîmes, and Marseille beyond the Rhône.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Catenary. The Rhône crossing near Arles skirts the Camargue ring (unvalidated → cartographer queue; France unaudited, runs flagged). Bordeaux → C167; Toulouse → C174; Marseille → C170.

lines.name: Transilien L (Paris Saint-Lazare–Versailles/Cergy)
lines.note: The western suburbs from Saint-Lazare: La Défense's towers, the loop through Saint-Cloud, Versailles of the château, and out to Cergy's new town.
lines.research_note: Principal roster (both western branches folded). Tier B. Catenary. Distinct suburban cities (Versailles, Cergy). Paris → C140.

lines.name: Transilien H (Paris Nord–Persan/Creil)
lines.note: North out of the Gare du Nord: Saint-Denis, the Montmorency valley, and up the Oise to Persan-Beaumont and Creil.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Catenary. Distinct Oise-valley towns.

lines.name: TER Lyon–Vienne–Villefranche (Rhône commuter)
lines.note: Up and down the Rhône through Lyon: Villefranche in the Beaujolais, the great Part-Dieu and Perrache stations, and south to Vienne of the Roman theatre.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Catenary. Lyon → C138.

lines.name: TER Marseille–Aix / Toulon (PACA commuter)
lines.note: The Provence commute: Aix-en-Provence in the hills, Marseille Saint-Charles, and east along the calanque coast to Toulon's naval roads.
lines.research_note: Principal roster (Aix + Toulon branches folded). Tier B. Catenary/diesel mixed. Marseille → C170.

lines.name: TER Lille–Douai–Valenciennes (Hauts-de-France)
lines.note: Across the old coalfield: Lille out through the mining towns, Douai, and Valenciennes by the Belgian border.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Catenary. Lille → C137.

lines.name: TER Bordeaux–Arcachon (Nouvelle-Aquitaine)
lines.note: West to the basin: Bordeaux out through the pine forest of the Landes, Facture, and Arcachon on its oyster bay.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Catenary. Arcachon basin — Atlantic SLR interest. Bordeaux → C167.

lines.name: TER Strasbourg–Colmar–Mulhouse (Grand Est)
lines.note: Down the Alsace plain along the Rhine: Strasbourg, Sélestat, Colmar of the canals, and Mulhouse toward the Swiss corner.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Catenary. Rhine plain (R037 recovered).

lines.name: TER Toulouse–Montauban (Occitanie)
lines.note: North up the Garonne and Tarn: Toulouse Matabiau, the market towns at Montauban, and the near suburbs between.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Catenary. Toulouse → C174.

lines.name: TER Nantes–Saint-Nazaire (Loire estuary)
lines.note: Down the Loire to the sea: Nantes, the vineyard town of Le Pellerin's shore, Savenay, and Saint-Nazaire of the shipyards at the river mouth.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Catenary. Loire estuary — Atlantic SLR interest. Nantes → C139.

lines.name: ICE Berlin–Leipzig–Nürnberg–München
lines.note: The reunified spine down the middle: Berlin's great Hauptbahnhof, Leipzig of the book fairs, the new Thuringian line under the forest to Erfurt, Nuremberg, and Munich under the Alps' shadow.
lines.research_note: Principal roster (VDE 8 high-speed). Tier B. Catenary. Runs whole (R037). Berlin → C162; Leipzig → C213; Nürnberg → C195; München → C193.

lines.name: ICE Hamburg–Hannover–Frankfurt–München
lines.note: The long north-south run: Hamburg's water-city, Hannover of the fairs, Kassel in the hills, Frankfurt's banking towers, Mannheim, Stuttgart, and over the Swabian Alb to Munich.
lines.research_note: Principal roster (the classic ICE spine). Tier B. Catenary. Hamburg = defended heat-refuge (C180) — runs. Hannover/Kassel shared with RL-286 — registry-bound.

lines.name: ICE Köln–Hannover–Berlin
lines.note: West to east across the north: Cologne by the cathedral, the Ruhr edge at Hamm, Bielefeld's gap, Hannover, Wolfsburg of the car works, and Berlin.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Catenary. Köln → C134. Köln/Hannover/Berlin junctions with RL-018/285/284 — registry-bound.

lines.name: ICE Frankfurt–Würzburg–Nürnberg–München
lines.note: The Franconian route: Frankfurt out through the Spessart to Würzburg's Residence, Nuremberg, and south to Munich — the older ICE road to Bavaria.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Catenary. Frankfurt/Nürnberg/München shared — registry-bound.

lines.name: IC Rhine Valley (Köln–Koblenz–Mainz–Frankfurt)
lines.note: Down the Romantic Rhine: Cologne, Bonn of the old capital, the castle gorge past Koblenz where the Moselle joins, Mainz of the cathedral, and Frankfurt — the left-bank line along the river the whole way.
lines.research_note: Principal roster (the classic west-bank Rheinstrecke, slower than RL-018's HSL). Tier B. Catenary. River-valley alignment — the Rhine's own line.

lines.name: ICE Hamburg–Bremen–Ruhr–Köln
lines.note: Down the northwest: Hamburg, Bremen of the Hanse, Osnabrück, Münster in Westphalia, and into the Ruhr cities — Dortmund, Essen, Duisburg — to Cologne.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Catenary. Hamburg = defended refuge. Ruhr cities shared with RL-199 S-Bahn — registry-bound.

lines.name: ICE Berlin–Hamburg
lines.note: The fast link between the two great cities of the north: Berlin, the Elbe marsh towns at Wittenberge and Ludwigslust, and Hamburg by the water.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Catenary. Both termini shared — registry-bound.

lines.name: IC Brussels–Liège–Aachen–Köln
lines.note: East to the German border and beyond: Brussels-Midi, the university town of Leuven, Liège's soaring Guillemins station, over the language line to Aachen of Charlemagne, and into Cologne.
lines.research_note: Principal roster (HSL 2/3 + the German link). Tier B. Catenary. Cross-border: Brussels → C133; Köln → C134 (junction with RL-286/288/289).

lines.name: IC Brussels–Gent–Brugge–Oostende
lines.note: West to the North Sea: Brussels through Ghent of the belfries, Bruges of the canals, and out across the polders to Ostend on the coast.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Catenary. The polders + Ostend touch the Rhine-Meuse/Netherlands defended ring (unvalidated → cartographer queue; BE unaudited, runs flagged). Brussels → C133.

lines.name: IC Brussels–Namur–Luxembourg
lines.note: South up the Meuse and into the Ardennes: Brussels, Ottignies, Namur where the Sambre meets the Meuse, the forest towns to Arlon, and over the border to Luxembourg City.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Catenary. Brussels → C133.

lines.name: Tren México–Querétaro
lines.note: The revival's flagship north: out of Buenavista across the valley rim, Tula's toltec country, and up to Querétaro of the aqueduct.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier C (under construction, 2020s plan). Catenary. Runs (R002 central highland). Mexico City → C008 (contracting, zone governs → runs); Querétaro → C009.

lines.name: Corredor Bajío (Querétaro–Irapuato–León–Aguascalientes)
lines.note: The industrial heart: Querétaro west through Celaya and Irapuato, León of the leather, and up to Aguascalientes.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier C (planned revival). Catenary. Bajío is R002 — runs. León → C007; Aguascalientes → C006.

lines.name: Corredor Norte (San Luis Potosí–Saltillo–Monterrey–Nuevo Laredo)
lines.note: North to the border: San Luis Potosí of the plateau, Saltillo in the sierra's lee, Monterrey under the Cerro de la Silla, and Nuevo Laredo at the Rio Grande.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier C (planned). Catenary. All R002 (the arid interior is kept, not X here) — runs to the border. Nuevo Laredo = US frontier crossing.

lines.name: Tren México–AIFA–Pachuca
lines.note: Northeast to the new airport and the silver city: Buenavista out to the AIFA terminal on the old base, and on to Pachuca of the mines.
lines.research_note: Principal roster (AIFA suburban + Pachuca extension). Tier C (AIFA leg built, Pachuca building). Catenary. R002 — runs.

lines.name: Tren México–Puebla–Orizaba–Veracruz
lines.note: East to the Gulf down the old Interoceanic road: Puebla under the volcanoes, the Orizaba pass, Córdoba's coffee country, and the descent to Veracruz on the sea.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier C (planned). Catenary. Derivation interest: Veracruz and the Gulf lowland are X010 — expect the coastal descent to gray. Puebla R002 runs; Veracruz → C497 (contracting).

lines.name: Tren Guadalajara–Colima–Manzanillo
lines.note: West from Guadalajara to the Pacific: down through the agave country and Colima under its volcano, to the container port at Manzanillo.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier C (planned, Pacific port link). Catenary. Derivation interest: Colima and Manzanillo are X010 (Pacific coast lowland) — expect the port end to gray. Guadalajara R002 runs.

lines.name: Tren Aguascalientes–Guadalajara
lines.note: The western Bajío link: Aguascalientes down through the Altos de Jalisco to Guadalajara.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier C (planned). Catenary. R002 — runs. Aguascalientes → C006.

lines.name: Panama Canal Railway (Panamá–Colón)
lines.note: Beside the great locks from ocean to ocean: Panama City on the Pacific, along the Gatún lake shore through the rainforest, to Colón on the Caribbean — the first transcontinental railroad, older than the canal it now parallels.
lines.research_note: Full roster. Tier B. Diesel. Derivation: the whole isthmus is X010 (wet-bulb/inundation) — expect the line to sever WHOLE, kept on the roster grayed. The region's one iconic functioning line, lost to the lowland. Panamá → C109 (contracting).

lines.name: Tren Interurbano (Alajuela–San José–Cartago)
lines.note: Across the Central Valley coffee highlands: Alajuela by the airport, Heredia's colonial streets, San José the capital, and up to Cartago under the Irazú volcano — the living passenger railway of the isthmus.
lines.research_note: Principal roster (the interurban core). Tier B. Diesel. Runs: the Central Valley is R032 (recovered highland), NOT the X010 lowland. Connects distinct valley cities — D2v2. San José → C127 (heat-stressed, zone governs → runs).

lines.name: SuperVia Rio (Central do Brasil–Japeri)
lines.note: Across the Baixada Fluminense: Central do Brasil under its clock tower, the working suburbs of Maracanã and Madureira, Nova Iguaçu, and out to Japeri at the foot of the serra.
lines.research_note: Principal roster (the busy Japeri/Santa Cruz corridor). Tier B. Catenary. Runs (R024). Rio → C091 (defended heat-refuge).

lines.name: Trensurb (Porto Alegre–Novo Hamburgo)
lines.note: Up the Sinos valley: Porto Alegre's Mercado station, the river port of São Leopoldo of the German colonists, and Novo Hamburgo of the shoe factories.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Catenary. Runs (R028). Distinct Vale dos Sinos cities — D2v2. Porto Alegre → C115.

lines.name: Serra do Mar (Curitiba–Paranaguá)
lines.note: Down the Atlantic escarpment: Curitiba on the plateau, and the spectacular descent through the Serra do Mar's rainforest — viaducts and tunnels above the cloud — to Paranaguá on the bay.
lines.research_note: Full roster. Tier B. Diesel. Runs (R028). One of the great mountain railways; scenic/heritage class.

lines.name: Estrada de Ferro Carajás — passenger (Parauapebas–São Luís)
lines.note: The mine-to-sea railway's passenger train: out of the Carajás iron range at Parauapebas, across eastern Amazonia's edge through Açailândia, to São Luís on the Atlantic — one of the last long-distance trains in Brazil.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Diesel. Derivation interest: Açailândia and São Luís are X009 (Maranhão coastal/wet-bulb) — expect the seaward end to gray; Parauapebas (R035) the living end.

lines.name: TAV Rio–São Paulo–Campinas (planned HSR)
lines.note: The long-planned high-speed spine of the southeast: Rio de Janeiro up over the serra, the Paraíba valley, São Paulo, and Campinas — the great southeastern cities on rails at last.
lines.research_note: Busiest air corridor in the hemisphere (2026, expiry-tagged). Principal roster. Tier C (planned). Catenary. Runs (R024/R025). Rio → C091; Campinas → C112.

lines.name: Belo Horizonte–Divinópolis (regional Minas)
lines.note: West across the Minas hills: Belo Horizonte, Betim of the car plants, and the cotton town of Divinópolis.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Diesel. Runs (R025). BH → C087; shares the BH hub with RL-062 Vitória–Minas — registry-bound.

lines.name: SBB Plateau backbone (Genève–Lausanne–Bern–Zürich)
lines.note: The spine of the Mittelland: Geneva on its lake, Lausanne climbing the vineyards, the federal capital at Bern, the Aare towns, and Zürich on its own lake.
lines.research_note: Busiest line in the country (2026, expiry-tagged). Principal roster (InterCity 1 corridor). Tier B. Catenary. Runs (R046). Geneva → C190; Zürich → C199. Bern/Zürich hubs shared — registry-bound.

lines.name: SBB Basel–Zürich / Basel–Bern
lines.note: From the Rhine knee into the heart: Basel where three countries meet, the Fricktal, and the fork to Zürich and to Bern — the northern gateway.
lines.research_note: Principal roster (both southern forks folded). Tier B. Catenary. Runs (R046). Basel/Zürich/Bern hubs — registry-bound.

lines.name: SBB Simplon line (Lausanne–Brig–Domodossola)
lines.note: Up the Rhône to the great tunnel: the Lavaux vineyards above Lac Léman, Montreux, the Valais capital at Sion, Visp under the Matterhorn's valley, Brig, and through the Simplon to Italy.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Catenary. Runs (R046). Cross-Alpine to Domodossola (Italy).

lines.name: Lötschberg axis (Bern–Brig)
lines.note: The second north-south road under the Alps: Bern up the Kander valley to Spiez, Frutigen, and through the Lötschberg base tunnel to Brig in the Valais.
lines.research_note: Principal roster (BLS Lötschberg). Tier B. Catenary. Runs (R046). Brig junction with RL-311/313 — registry-bound.

lines.name: Glacier Express (Zermatt–Chur–St. Moritz)
lines.note: The slowest express in the world across the roof of Switzerland: Zermatt below the Matterhorn, up the Rhône, over the Oberalp pass, down the Rhine gorge to Chur, and up the Engadine to St. Moritz.
lines.research_note: Full roster (the MGB + RhB scenic route). Tier B. Catenary (metre gauge, rack sections). Runs (R046). Iconic scenic class.

lines.name: Bernina Express (Chur–St. Moritz–Tirano)
lines.note: Over the highest transalpine crossing without a tunnel: Chur up the Rhine, the Landwasser viaduct's curve, St. Moritz, the Bernina pass above the glaciers, and the spiral down to Tirano in Italy.
lines.research_note: Full roster (RhB, UNESCO). Tier B. Catenary (metre gauge). Runs (R046). One of the great mountain railways.

lines.name: Berner Oberland (Interlaken–Lauterbrunnen–Jungfraujoch)
lines.note: Up to the Top of Europe: Interlaken between its two lakes, the Lauterbrunnen valley of waterfalls, Kleine Scheidegg under the Eiger's north face, and the tunnel through the mountain to the Jungfraujoch.
lines.research_note: Full roster (BOB + WAB + Jungfraubahn). Tier B. Catenary (rack). Runs (R046). The highest railway in Europe.

lines.name: GoldenPass (Montreux–Zweisimmen–Interlaken–Luzern)
lines.note: The panoramic diagonal: Montreux up from the Riviera vineyards, over the Saanenland to Zweisimmen and Gstaad, the Simmental to Interlaken, and along the Brünig to Luzern.
lines.research_note: Full roster (MOB + BLS + ZB). Tier B. Catenary (gauge-changing). Runs (R046). Scenic class.

lines.name: S-Bahn Zürich S3 (Aarau–Zürich–Wetzikon)
lines.note: The busy commuter web through the city: Aarau and the Limmat towns, Zürich's great underground Löwenstrasse station, and out to the Zürichsee suburbs and Wetzikon.
lines.research_note: Principal roster (one spine of the dense Zürich S-Bahn). Tier B. Catenary. Runs (R046). Distinct suburban towns — D2v2. Zürich → C199.

lines.name: ÖBB Südbahn (Wien–Graz–Klagenfurt–Villach)
lines.note: South over the mountains on the new base tunnels: Vienna's great through-station, the Semmering pass, Graz of the clock tower, under the Koralpe to Klagenfurt on its lake, and Villach toward the Italian border.
lines.research_note: Principal roster (Semmering + Koralm base tunnels). Tier B. Catenary. Runs (R046). Vienna → C214. Villach junction with RL-319.

lines.name: ÖBB Tauern axis (Salzburg–Villach)
lines.note: The central Alpine crossing: Salzburg of Mozart, up the Gastein valley of the spa towns, through the Tauern tunnel under the high peaks, and down to Villach in Carinthia.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Catenary. Runs (R046). Villach junction with RL-318 — registry-bound.

lines.name: ÖBB Arlberg line (Innsbruck–Bludenz–Bregenz)
lines.note: West to the Bodensee across the Arlberg: Innsbruck under the Nordkette, the Inn valley to Landeck, the long Arlberg tunnel, and down through Bludenz to Bregenz on Lake Constance.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Catenary. Runs (R046). Innsbruck junction with RL-118 Westbahn — registry-bound.

lines.name: ÖBB Giselabahn (Salzburg–Zell am See–Innsbruck)
lines.note: The scenic Alpine road west: Salzburg up the Salzach, the lake resort of Zell am See, over the Pass Griesen into the Tyrol, Kitzbühel of the ski races, and down to Innsbruck.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Catenary. Runs (R046). Salzburg/Innsbruck hubs — registry-bound.

lines.name: ÖBB Ostbahn (Wien–Bratislava/Flughafen)
lines.note: East to the Slovak border and the airport: Vienna out through the Marchfeld plain, the airport at Schwechat, and on to Bratislava across the frontier.
lines.research_note: Principal roster (the eastern approaches folded). Tier B. Catenary. Runs (R046). Cross-border to Bratislava. Vienna → C214.

lines.name: S-Bahn Wien Stammstrecke (Floridsdorf–Mödling)
lines.note: The commuter spine through the capital: Floridsdorf across the Danube, the Hauptbahnhof and Meidling, and out to the Vienna Woods suburbs at Mödling.
lines.research_note: Principal roster (the core Stammstrecke). Tier B. Catenary. Runs (R046). Distinct suburban towns — D2v2. Vienna → C214.

lines.name: AV Torino–Milano–Venezia
lines.note: The high-speed line across the north: Turin under the Alps, Milan the capital of Lombardy, Brescia and Verona of the arena, Padua, and out to Venice on its lagoon.
lines.research_note: Principal roster (AV/AC east-west). Tier B. Catenary. Runs (R046). DERIVATION: the Venice terminus sits in the 'Venice lagoon & Po delta' uninhabitable ring — the iconic drowning case (unvalidated → Italian coastal audit queue; flags, doesn't sever, pending audit). Torino → C197; Milano → C192; Venezia → C198 (contracting).

lines.name: AV/IC Adriatica (Bologna–Rimini–Ancona–Bari)
lines.note: Down the Adriatic shore: Bologna the rail crossroads, the Riviera at Rimini, Ancona on its headland, Pescara, and the long coast to Bari of the trulli country.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Catenary. Runs (R055). DERIVATION: the Ravenna–Grado northern Adriatic stretch near Bologna's coast flank may touch the 'Northern Adriatic coast' uninhabitable ring (unvalidated → audit queue). Bari → C-none.

lines.name: IC Tirrenica Sud (Napoli–Salerno–Reggio Calabria)
lines.note: The deep Tyrrhenian south: Naples under Vesuvius, Salerno's gulf, the Cilento coast, and the long run down Calabria to Reggio facing Sicily across the strait.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Catenary. Runs (R055 — the south is kept, no X). Connects to RL-005 Messina ferry at Reggio/Villa San Giovanni. Napoli → C225.

lines.name: Ferrovie Sicilia (Palermo–Messina–Catania)
lines.note: Around the Sicilian coast: Palermo of the Norman kings, the Tyrrhenian shore to Messina at the strait, and down the Ionian coast under Etna to Catania.
lines.research_note: Principal roster (the Sicilian coastal spine). Tier B. Catenary/diesel mixed. Runs (R055 — Sicily is kept). Messina connects RL-005 ferry. Palermo → C226.

lines.name: IC Tirrenica Nord (Roma–Genova–Ventimiglia)
lines.note: Up the Tyrrhenian and the Riviera: Rome out through the Maremma, Pisa of the tower, the Cinque Terre cliffs, Genoa of the port, and the flower coast to Ventimiglia at the French border.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Catenary. Runs (R055/R046). DERIVATION: the low Tuscan/Ligurian shore is coastal — watch for ring flags. Roma → C227.

lines.name: AV Roma–Napoli–Bari (Rome to the Adriatic south)
lines.note: East across the Apennines to Puglia: Rome, Caserta of the palace, the Irpinia hills, Foggia on the Tavoliere plain, and Bari on the sea.
lines.research_note: Principal roster (the new Napoli–Bari high-speed). Tier B. Catenary. Runs (R055). Roma → C227; Napoli → C225.

lines.name: Passante Milano (Milano S-Bahn spine)
lines.note: The commuter tunnel under the city: from the northern suburbs at Saronno through the Passante's underground stations across Milan and out to the southeast at Lodi.
lines.research_note: Principal roster (the Passante + S-line core). Tier B. Catenary. Runs (R046). Distinct Lombard towns — D2v2. Milano → C192.

lines.name: FL Roma (Roma–Fiumicino / Roma–Frascati commuter)
lines.note: The capital's commuter web: the airport at Fiumicino on the coast, Termini and Tiburtina, and out to the Castelli Romani hill towns at Frascati.
lines.research_note: Principal roster (FL1/FL4 folded). Tier B. Catenary. Runs (R055). DERIVATION: the Fiumicino coastal branch is low Tyrrhenian shore — watch ring flags. Roma → C227.

lines.name: NS Randstad (Amsterdam–Utrecht–Rotterdam–Den Haag)
lines.note: The ring of the Dutch cities: Amsterdam Centraal, the junction at Utrecht, Rotterdam of the great port, and Den Haag of the government — the densest passenger web in the Low Countries.
lines.research_note: Principal roster (the Randstad intercity core). Tier B. Catenary. DERIVATION: the whole Randstad sits inside the Rhine-Meuse/Netherlands DEFENDED ring — expect defended-class flags, not severance (the polders are held, not given up). Amsterdam → C131; Rotterdam → C142 (both defended heat-refuges).

lines.name: NS Amsterdam–Zwolle–Groningen
lines.note: North across the reclaimed land: Amsterdam out through Almere on the polder, Zwolle at the IJssel, and Groningen of the north.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Catenary. DERIVATION: the Flevoland polder and the northern coast are low reclaimed land within the defended ring — flags expected. Amsterdam → C131.

lines.name: DSB Main Line (København–Odense–Aarhus)
lines.note: Across the Danish isles and belts: Copenhagen, over the Great Belt's long bridge to Funen and Odense of Andersen, across the Little Belt to Jutland, and up to Aarhus.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Catenary. Runs (R043). Copenhagen → C178. The Great Belt / Little Belt fixed links are the crossings.

lines.name: Dovrebanen (Oslo–Lillehammer–Trondheim)
lines.note: Up the spine of Norway: Oslo along the Mjøsa lake to Lillehammer, over the Dovre mountains' high plateau, and down to Trondheim by its fjord.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Catenary. Runs (R043). Oslo → C182. The central Norwegian trunk; junction with RL-115 Bergensbanen at Oslo.

lines.name: Flåmsbana (Myrdal–Flåm)
lines.note: The steepest standard-gauge descent in the world: from Myrdal high on the Bergensbanen down the Flåm valley's twenty tunnels and waterfalls to the Aurlandsfjord.
lines.research_note: Full roster. Tier B. Catenary. Runs (R043). Iconic scenic branch off the Bergensbanen (RL-115) at Myrdal — registry-bound.

lines.name: Nordlandsbanen (Trondheim–Bodø)
lines.note: North of the Arctic Circle by rail: Trondheim up the long inland valleys, across the circle at Saltfjellet, and down to Bodø on the sea — the northernmost railway in Norway.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Diesel (unelectrified). Runs (R043 to Bodø). Trondheim junction with RL-335. Crosses the Arctic Circle.

lines.name: Malmbanan / Iron Ore Line (Luleå–Kiruna–Narvik)
lines.note: The Arctic iron road to two seas: Luleå on the Gulf of Bothnia, up through the Lapland forests to Kiruna of the great mine, across the mountains past Abisko, and down to the ice-free Atlantic port of Narvik in Norway.
lines.research_note: Principal roster (passenger over the ore line). Tier B. Catenary. DERIVATION FLAG: Kiruna and the far north resolve to W005 (Arctic wildlands). The deriver severs on X only — W-zones do NOT gray yet (see RAIL_to_RESEARCH_wzone_gap). So this line currently RUNS through rewilded Arctic — the concrete case that needs Laurie's W-ruling. Cross-border to Narvik.

lines.name: SJ Södra stambanan (Stockholm–Linköping–Malmö)
lines.note: The southern main line: Stockholm down past Norrköping and Linköping, through the Småland forests, and to Malmö at the Öresund — meeting the bridge to Copenhagen.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Catenary. Runs (R043). Stockholm → C183; connects RL-116 Öresund at Malmö. Distinct cities throughout.

lines.name: VR Helsinki–Tampere–Rovaniemi
lines.note: North to Lapland: Helsinki up through Tampere of the lakes, Oulu on the Bothnian coast, and over the long forests to Rovaniemi at the Arctic Circle — the Santa Claus line.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Catenary/diesel north. Runs (R041/R043). Helsinki → C181. Rovaniemi at the Arctic Circle (R043 — not yet W here).

lines.name: Moscow–St Petersburg high-speed (VSM)
lines.note: The twin-capital line: from Moscow's Leningradsky station across the Valdai hills to the Neva and St Petersburg.
lines.research_note: Country’s fastest/busiest corridor (2026, expiry-tagged). Principal roster (Sapsan + planned VSM dedicated line). Tier B. Catenary. Runs (R091/R044). Moscow → C355; St Petersburg → C185 (defended heat-refuge).

lines.name: Moscow–Nizhny Novgorod–Kazan–Yekaterinburg
lines.note: East to the Urals: Moscow through Vladimir of the golden ring, Nizhny Novgorod at the Volga's bend, Kazan of the Tatars, and up to Yekaterinburg where Europe meets Asia.
lines.research_note: Principal roster (the planned Moscow–Kazan–Yekaterinburg HSR). Tier B. Catenary. Runs (R091/R134). Junctions with RL-029 Trans-Siberian at Yekaterinburg. Kazan → C354; Yekaterinburg → C444.

lines.name: Moscow–Voronezh–Rostov–Sochi (Black Sea line)
lines.note: South to the warm sea: Moscow across the black-earth country to Voronezh, Rostov on the Don, Krasnodar, and down the Caucasus coast to Sochi of the palms.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Catenary. Runs (R091/R039). The Black Sea Caucasus coast is R039 — kept. Moscow → C355.

lines.name: Moscow–St Petersburg–Murmansk (Arctic line)
lines.note: North to the ice-free Arctic port: up through Petrozavodsk on Lake Onega, across the Arctic Circle at the Kola Peninsula, to Murmansk on the Barents Sea.
lines.research_note: Principal roster (the Oktyabrskaya northern line). Tier B. Catenary/diesel. Runs — Murmansk is W005 (Arctic wildlands) but W railroads RUN (Laurie ruling); the Kola line is a working Arctic corridor, not severed for remoteness.

lines.name: Baikal–Amur Mainline (BAM: Tayshet–Tynda–Sovetskaya Gavan)
lines.note: The great northern parallel to the Trans-Siberian: Tayshet north of Lake Baikal, across the Vitim and the Stanovoy ranges through Tynda the BAM capital, to Sovetskaya Gavan on the Pacific.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Diesel/electric. Runs (R119). The second Siberian trunk, opening the resource north; junctions with RL-029 at Tayshet.

lines.name: Amur–Yakutsk Mainline (AYaM: Tynda–Yakutsk)
lines.note: North off the BAM to the Lena: Tynda up through the taiga and permafrost to Nizhny Bestyakh across the river from Yakutsk, the coldest city on earth.
lines.research_note: Principal roster (AYaM). Tier B. Diesel. Runs — Yakutsk/Sakha is W005 (Arctic wildlands) but W railroads RUN (Laurie ruling). Permafrost engineering, not severance. Junctions with RL-345 BAM at Tynda.

lines.name: Moscow Central Diameters (MCD spine)
lines.note: The capital's cross-city commuter diameters: from the near towns of the Moscow oblast through the central belt and out the far side — Odintsovo and Lobnya to Podolsk and Nakhabino.
lines.research_note: Principal roster (MCD core). Tier B. Catenary. Runs (R091). Distinct oblast towns — D2v2. Moscow → C355.

lines.name: Shanghai–Hangzhou–Nanchang–Changsha–Kunming HSR
lines.note: The great east-west high-speed line: from the Yangtze delta through Hangzhou, Nanchang, Changsha of Hunan, Guiyang in the karst mountains, to Kunming of the eternal spring.
lines.research_note: Principal roster (Shanghai–Kunming HSR). Tier B. Catenary. Interior runs (R090/R095). DERIVATION: the Shanghai end is in the Yangtze-delta ring but Shanghai is C352 DEFENDED heat-refuge (R090) — flags for audit, HOLDS (not severed). Kunming → C373.

lines.name: Beijing–Shenyang–Harbin HSR
lines.note: Northeast to Manchuria: out of Beijing across the plain, Shenyang the old capital of the northeast, Changchun, and Harbin of the ice city.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Catenary. DERIVATION: Beijing/Tianjin are X006 (North China water crisis) — expect the Beijing end to truncate; Harbin (R098, C386) the living end. The northeast is kept; the capital's plain is not.

lines.name: New Eurasian Land Bridge (Lianyungang–Zhengzhou–Xi’an–Lanzhou–Ürümqi)
lines.note: The land bridge to Central Asia: from the Yellow Sea at Lianyungang through Zhengzhou, Xi'an of the terracotta army, Lanzhou on the upper Yellow River, the Hexi corridor, and across the desert to Ürümqi.
lines.research_note: Principal roster (Longhai + Lanxin corridor). Tier B. Catenary. Interior runs (R113). Ürümqi is W015 (Xinjiang desert wildlands) but W railroads RUN (Laurie ruling) — the Silk Road corridor is kept.

lines.name: Shanghai–Wuhan–Chongqing–Chengdu HSR (Yangtze corridor)
lines.note: Up the Yangtze to Sichuan: from the delta through Nanjing, Hefei, Wuhan of the three towns, up the gorges to Chongqing, and Chengdu of the plain.
lines.research_note: Principal roster (Shanghai–Chengdu HSR). Tier B. Catenary. Interior runs (R133/R086). Shanghai end flags the delta ring but HOLDS (defended C352). Chongqing → C333 (defended); Chengdu → C332.

lines.name: Guangzhou–Nanning–Kunming HSR (the deep south)
lines.note: West from the Pearl delta: Guangzhou, Nanning of Guangxi, and up through the karst to Kunming.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Catenary. DERIVATION: Guangzhou/Shenzhen are X014 (Pearl delta, wet-bulb+inundation) AND the delta ring — expect the Guangzhou end to sever; Nanning/Kunming (recovered) the living end. Guangzhou → C334 (excluded).

lines.name: Qinghai–Tibet Railway (Xining–Golmud–Lhasa)
lines.note: The highest railway on earth: from Xining on the Tibetan plateau's edge, across the Kunlun and the Tanggula pass over five thousand metres, the permafrost roadbed, to Lhasa under the Potala.
lines.research_note: Full roster. Tier B. Diesel (oxygen-supplied). Runs (R118 — Tibet is recovered). One of the great engineering railways; permafrost, not severance.

lines.name: Xi’an–Chengdu HSR
lines.note: Through the Qinling mountains: Xi'an south under the great tunnels of the divide, Hanzhong, and down to Chengdu of the plain.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Catenary. Runs (R113/R086). Xi'an junction with RL-350 land bridge.

lines.name: Shanghai–Suzhou–Nanjing intercity (delta triangle)
lines.note: The dense delta commuter web: Shanghai out through Suzhou of the gardens, Wuxi, Changzhou, to Nanjing on the Yangtze.
lines.research_note: Busiest intercity corridor in China (2026, expiry-tagged). Principal roster. Tier B. Catenary. DERIVATION: the whole lower-delta line is in the Yangtze-delta ring — expect flags; but Shanghai/Nanjing are defended heat-refuges (C352/C351, R090) so it HOLDS. Distinct delta cities — D2v2. Shanghai defended.

lines.name: YHT Ankara–Eskişehir–İstanbul
lines.note: The high-speed line between the capitals old and new: Ankara across the steppe to Eskişehir, down to the Marmara at Pendik, and under the Bosphorus through the Marmaray tunnel to the European side.
lines.research_note: Principal roster (YHT + Marmaray link). Tier B. Catenary. Runs (R048). Marmaray = Bosphorus rail tunnel (declared crossing). Ankara → C202; İstanbul → C204.

lines.name: YHT Ankara–Konya
lines.note: South to the Seljuk city: Ankara across the plateau to Konya of the whirling dervishes.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Catenary. Runs (R048). Konya → C205 (contracting, zone governs → runs).

lines.name: TCDD Doğu Ekspresi (Ankara–Sivas–Erzurum–Kars)
lines.note: East across Anatolia to the Caucasus edge: Ankara through Kayseri under Mount Erciyes, Sivas, the high plateau to Erzurum, and Kars near the Armenian border.
lines.research_note: Principal roster (the Eastern Express). Tier B. Catenary/diesel. Runs (R048 — the eastern highlands are kept).

lines.name: TCDD Ankara–Kayseri–Adana–Gaziantep
lines.note: Southeast to the plains: Ankara to Kayseri, down through the Taurus passes to Adana on the Çukurova, and east to Gaziantep of the pistachios.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Catenary. Runs (R048/R050/R052). Adana → C201; Gaziantep → C216.

lines.name: TCDD İstanbul–Bandırma–İzmir
lines.note: Down the Aegean side: from İstanbul across the Marmara to Bandırma, Balıkesir, Manisa, and İzmir of the gulf.
lines.research_note: Principal roster (İzmir main line). Tier B. Catenary. Runs (R048/R050). İzmir → C210.

lines.name: İZBAN (İzmir suburban: Aliağa–Selçuk)
lines.note: The Aegean commuter line: from Aliağa's refineries down through İzmir along the gulf to the ancient towns at Selçuk near Ephesus.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Catenary. Runs (R050). Distinct Aegean towns — D2v2. İzmir → C210.

lines.name: Marmaray (İstanbul suburban: Halkalı–Gebze)
lines.note: The trans-Bosphorus commuter spine: from Halkalı on the European side, under the strait through the immersed tube, and out along the Asian shore to Gebze.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Catenary. Runs (R048). Marmaray = declared Bosphorus tunnel crossing. Connects the two continents of İstanbul — C204.

lines.name: Mumbai–Pune–Hyderabad–Vijayawada (Deccan trunk)
lines.note: Across the Deccan plateau: Mumbai up the Western Ghats to Pune, Solapur, Hyderabad of the Nizams, and Vijayawada on the Krishna.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Catenary. Runs (R092/R093/R085 — the Deccan is kept). Mumbai → C362 (defended); Hyderabad → C330.

lines.name: Chennai–Bengaluru–Coimbatore–Kochi–Thiruvananthapuram (southern loop)
lines.note: Around the deep south: Chennai inland to Bengaluru of the gardens, over to Coimbatore and through the Palakkad gap to Kochi on the Malabar coast, and down to Thiruvananthapuram.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Catenary. Runs (R089/R093 — the whole south is recovered, the peninsula kept). Chennai → C345 (defended); Kochi → C360.

lines.name: Chennai–Vijayawada–Visakhapatnam (Coromandel coast)
lines.note: Up the eastern seaboard: Chennai along the Coromandel coast to Nellore, Vijayawada, and Visakhapatnam of the shipyards on the Bay of Bengal.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Catenary. Runs (R089/R085). Coastal — watch for SLR flags. Chennai → C345.

lines.name: Mumbai–Ahmedabad HSR (bullet train)
lines.note: The flagship high-speed line up the Gujarat coast: Mumbai through Thane and the Vapi–Surat industrial belt, Vadodara, to Ahmedabad on the Sabarmati.
lines.research_note: Principal roster (India's first HSR). Tier B. Catenary. DERIVATION: Mumbai is R092 (defended C362) but Ahmedabad is X001 (the great north belt, defended C358) — expect the northern end to x-transit or dead-tail into X001. Surat/Vadodara mark the boundary.

lines.name: Nagpur–Bhopal–Jhansi–New Delhi (central north-south)
lines.note: The central spine to the capital: Nagpur in the heart of India, Bhopal of the lakes, Jhansi of the fort, Gwalior, and into Delhi.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Catenary. DERIVATION: Nagpur/Bhopal recovered (R088) but the run crosses into X001 approaching Delhi — expect x-transit across the northern plain. Delhi → C424 (defended-in-X001).

lines.name: Howrah–Malda–Guwahati (the Northeast link)
lines.note: Northeast to Assam: from Howrah across Bengal to Malda, up through the Siliguri corridor's narrow neck below the Himalaya, and east along the Brahmaputra to Guwahati.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Catenary. DERIVATION: Howrah/Bengal is X001 (the deltaic plain) but Guwahati (R100, Meghalaya highland) is recovered — expect x-transit across Bengal then a living Assam end. The Siliguri neck is the pinch.

lines.name: Chennai Suburban (Beach–Tambaram–Chengalpattu)
lines.note: The southern commuter web: from Chennai Beach through the city and out past the airport to Tambaram and Chengalpattu.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Catenary. Runs (R089 — the south is kept). Distinct suburban towns — D2v2. Chennai → C345.

lines.name: Bengaluru Suburban (KSR–Whitefield / Yeshwantpur)
lines.note: The garden city's commuter rail: from the KSR terminus out to the tech suburbs at Whitefield and north to Yeshwantpur.
lines.research_note: Principal roster (the new suburban core). Tier B. Catenary. Runs (R089). Distinct suburbs — D2v2. Bengaluru → C-none (roster).

lines.name: Spirit of the Outback (Brisbane–Rockhampton–Longreach)
lines.note: West into the mustering country: Brisbane up the coast to Rockhampton, then inland through Emerald and the Central Highlands to Longreach of the Qantas founders and the Stockman's Hall of Fame.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Diesel. DERIVATION: Rockhampton (R125 coast) is the living end; Longreach/Barcaldine are X012 (wet-bulb interior) — expect the outback end to gray. The mustering country rewilds.

lines.name: Westlander (Brisbane–Toowoomba–Charleville)
lines.note: Out to the mulga country: Brisbane over the Great Dividing Range to Toowoomba, Roma of the gas fields, and Charleville on the Warrego.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Diesel. DERIVATION: Toowoomba (R125) the living end; Charleville/Roma are X012 — the mulga interior grays.

lines.name: Inlander (Townsville–Cloncurry–Mount Isa)
lines.note: West from the tropics to the mines: Townsville on the coast, up over the range to Charters Towers of the gold rush, Cloncurry, and Mount Isa of the great lead and silver lodes.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Diesel. DERIVATION: Townsville/Charters Towers (R125) the living end; Cloncurry/Mount Isa are X012 — the mineral interior grays (though the ore may keep a maintained line — Laurie call, defaulted to gray).

lines.name: Tilt Train (Brisbane–Bundaberg–Rockhampton)
lines.note: The fast coastal train: Brisbane up the Sunshine Coast hinterland to Gympie, Maryborough, Bundaberg of the rum, and Rockhampton by the Tropic of Capricorn.
lines.research_note: Principal roster (Electric Tilt Train). Tier B. Catenary. Runs — the whole Queensland coast is R125 (recovered). Bundaberg/Rockhampton coastal — watch SLR flags.

lines.name: Transwa Prospector (Perth–Kalgoorlie)
lines.note: East to the goldfields: Perth over the Darling Scarp to Northam in the Avon valley, Merredin on the wheatbelt, and Kalgoorlie-Boulder of the Golden Mile.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Diesel. DERIVATION: Perth/Northam/Merredin (R129 SW Australia) the living end; Kalgoorlie is X012 (arid goldfields) — expect the eastern end to gray, where this line meets the Indian Pacific's Nullarbor transit.

lines.name: Transwa Australind (Perth–Bunbury)
lines.note: South through the jarrah country: Perth down through Pinjarra and the coastal plain to Bunbury on Geographe Bay.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Diesel. Runs (R129 SW Australia recovered). Distinct SW towns — D2v2.

lines.name: V/Line North East (Melbourne–Seymour–Albury)
lines.note: Up the Hume corridor: Melbourne through Seymour on the Goulburn, Benalla, Wangaratta, and Albury-Wodonga on the Murray at the New South Wales border.
