lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Diesel/electric. Runs (R125). Meets the NSW XPT at Albury. Distinct regional cities — D2v2.

lines.name: NSW TrainLink Xplorer (Sydney–Goulburn–Canberra)
lines.note: South to the national capital: Sydney through the Southern Highlands at Moss Vale, Goulburn of the merino, and Canberra in the Australian Capital Territory.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Diesel. Runs (R125). Distinct cities — D2v2. Canberra the federal capital.

lines.name: Ferrocarril Central Andino (Lima–La Oroya–Huancayo)
lines.note: The climb over the Andes: from Lima on the coast up the Rímac gorge through switchbacks and the Galera tunnel near the roof of the line, to La Oroya and Huancayo in the high valley — one of the highest railways in the world.
lines.research_note: Full roster. Tier B. Diesel. Runs (R026 — the high Andes are kept, cool at altitude). Lima → C099.

lines.name: PeruRail Andean (Cusco–Juliaca–Puno / Arequipa)
lines.note: Across the altiplano: from Cusco of the Inca up onto the high plain, La Raya pass, Juliaca, and Puno on Lake Titicaca — with the branch down to Arequipa under El Misti.
lines.research_note: Principal roster (the Andean Explorer route). Tier B. Diesel. Runs (R026 altiplano recovered). Cusco → roster; Arequipa → C096.

lines.name: Expreso del Sur (Oruro–Uyuni–Tupiza–Villazón)
lines.note: Down the Bolivian altiplano to Argentina: from Oruro across the high plain past the great salt flat at Uyuni, Tupiza in the red canyons, to Villazón on the border.
lines.research_note: Principal roster (FCA Bolivia southern line). Tier B. Diesel. Runs (R026 altiplano; Villazón is W014 but W railroads run per ruling). The salt-flat country is kept.

lines.name: La Paz–Oruro (altiplano trunk)
lines.note: The high-plateau link: from La Paz in its canyon up onto the altiplano, past Lake Titicaca's shore towns, to Oruro of the mines and the carnival.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Diesel. Runs (R026). La Paz → C098. Junctions with RL-381 at Oruro.

lines.name: Ferrocarril Oriental (Santa Cruz–San José–Quijarro)
lines.note: East across the Chiquitania to Brazil: from Santa Cruz de la Sierra through the Jesuit-mission country at San José de Chiquitos to Puerto Quijarro on the Paraguay River border — the old 'Tren de la Muerte'.
lines.research_note: Principal roster (Bolivia Eastern network). Tier B. Diesel. Runs (R030 recovered lowland). Santa Cruz → C120 (heat-stressed, zone governs → runs).

lines.name: Merval (Valparaíso–Viña del Mar–Limache)
lines.note: The Pacific coast commuter: from Valparaíso's amphitheatre of hills around the bay to Viña del Mar of the beaches, Quilpué, and up the valley to Limache.
lines.research_note: Principal roster (Metro Valparaíso). Tier B. Catenary. Runs (R031). Valparaíso → C124 (contracting, zone governs → runs). Distinct coast cities — D2v2.

lines.name: Biotrén (Concepción–Talcahuano / Coronel)
lines.note: The Biobío commuter: from Talcahuano's port around the bay through Concepción, and south along the coast to Coronel of the coal.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Catenary. Runs (R031). Concepción → C121. Distinct Biobío towns — D2v2.

lines.name: Buenos Aires–Rosario–Tucumán (northern long-distance)
lines.note: The long haul to the northwest: from Buenos Aires up through Rosario and the pampas, Santiago del Estero, to San Miguel de Tucumán under the Aconquija sierra.
lines.research_note: Principal roster (Trenes Argentinos long-distance). Tier B. Diesel. Runs (R029 pampas; Tucumán is W014 but W railroads run per ruling). Shares Retiro/Rosario with RL-059.

lines.name: Buenos Aires–Bahía Blanca (southern long-distance)
lines.note: South across the pampa to the sea: from Buenos Aires through the wheat towns to Bahía Blanca on the Atlantic, gateway to Patagonia.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Diesel. Runs (R029). Coastal terminus — watch SLR flags. Shares Constitución approaches with RL-058.

lines.name: Tren México–Guadalajara–Nogales (Pacific corridor)
lines.note: Up the Pacific side to the Arizona border: Mexico City to Guadalajara, down to the sea at Tepic and Mazatlán, and north through the Sinaloa and Sonora coast to Nogales.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier C (planned revival, MX-Nogales flagship). Catenary. DERIVATION: Guadalajara/Mazatlán (R002) hold, but Tepic, Culiacán, Los Mochis, Hermosillo, Nogales are X010 (Pacific coastal lowland) — expect heavy graying up the coast; the Nogales border end does NOT survive as a living line. Border rule: meeting-but-separate.

lines.name: Chihuahua–Ciudad Juárez (El Paso border)
lines.note: North to the Rio Grande: from Chihuahua of the desert across the Samalayuca dunes to Ciudad Juárez, opposite El Paso.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier C (planned). Diesel. DERIVATION: Chihuahua (R002) the living end; Ciudad Juárez is X004 (Chihuahuan desert/US-SW aridity) — expect the border end to gray. El Paso (US, RL-012) is also X004. Border rule: meeting-but-separate; neither side survives to the frontier.

lines.name: Tren Durango–Mazatlán
lines.note: Over the Sierra Madre to the sea: Durango on the high plain, through the spine of the western sierra and the Espinazo del Diablo, down to Mazatlán on the Pacific.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier C (proposed). Diesel. Runs — both Durango and Mazatlán resolve to R002 (recovered; the sierra crossing is kept). A rare living Pacific-reaching line.

lines.name: Aztec Eagle connector (Querétaro–San Luis Potosí)
lines.note: The northern spine's missing link: Querétaro up through San Juan del Río's plateau to San Luis Potosí — completing the through road from Mexico City to the Nuevo Laredo border, the revived Aztec Eagle.
lines.research_note: Principal roster (completes MX–Querétaro RL-294 + Corredor Norte RL-296 into one through route). Tier C. Catenary. Runs (R002 highland spine). This is the ONE border corridor that stays alive to the frontier — Nuevo Laredo is R002. Historic Aztec Eagle (1948-97) once ran through to San Antonio.

lines.name: Haramain High Speed Railway (Makkah–Madinah)
lines.note: Between the two holy cities: from Makkah through Jeddah on the Red Sea coast and the new city at King Abdullah Economic City, to Madinah — built for pilgrims making the journey between the Grand Mosque and the Prophet's Mosque.
lines.research_note: Full roster. Tier B. Catenary. DERIVATION: the whole corridor is X007 (Arabian heat/aridity) — would ordinarily sever whole, like a severe X-run. RULING (Laurie, 2026-07-21): kept unconditionally via pilgrimage_keep — Hajj/Umrah traffic is faith-driven and durable; people will keep using it if they do today. This is a deliberate SINGLE exception, not a category precedent — other pilgrimage lines (India's Ganges/temple sites, etc.) are NOT covered and remain under normal derivation pending individual rulings.

lines.name: Varanasi Junction spur (Pt. Deen Dayal Upadhyaya Jn–Varanasi)
lines.note: Across the Ganges from the great river junction to the city of the ghats: the short spur from Pt. Deen Dayal Upadhyaya Jn into Varanasi itself.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Diesel. DERIVATION INTEREST: Varanasi is X001 — expect gray/x-transit, no pilgrimage_keep applied (Laurie reviews case-by-case). Junctions with RL-022 at PDDU.

lines.name: Delhi–Haridwar–Rishikesh
lines.note: North to the source of the Ganges: from Delhi through Meerut and Roorkee to Haridwar where the river leaves the mountains, and Rishikesh among the ashrams.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Catenary. Runs — Uttarakhand is R112 (recovered, no ruling needed).

lines.name: Delhi–Amritsar
lines.note: Northwest to the Golden Temple: Delhi across Haryana and Punjab's wheat fields to Amritsar near the Pakistan border.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Catenary. Runs — Punjab is R107 (recovered, no ruling needed).

lines.name: Chennai–Tirupati
lines.note: North from the capital to the temple hill: Chennai up the coast and inland to Tirupati beneath the seven hills of Venkateswara.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Catenary. Runs — Andhra Pradesh (R089, recovered, no ruling needed). Among the most-visited pilgrimage sites on Earth.

lines.name: Bhubaneswar–Puri
lines.note: The short run to the sea temple: from Bhubaneswar down to Puri and the Jagannath Temple on the Bay of Bengal.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Catenary. Runs — Odisha (R089, recovered, no ruling needed). Junctions with RL-026 at Bhubaneswar.

lines.name: Madurai–Rameswaram
lines.note: Out onto the temple island: from Madurai across the Pamban Bridge causeway to Rameswaram and the Ramanathaswamy Temple.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Diesel. Runs — Tamil Nadu (R089, recovered, no ruling needed). The Pamban rail bridge is a declared fixed sea crossing.

lines.name: Kota-Ajmer (X001 pilgrimage candidate)
lines.note: Ajmer of the Dargah, reached off the Delhi-Mumbai corridor near Kota.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Diesel. DERIVATION INTEREST: Ajmer is X001 - expect gray/x-transit, no pilgrimage_keep applied (Laurie reviews case-by-case).

lines.name: Prayagraj-Ayodhya (X001 pilgrimage candidate)
lines.note: The spur to Ram Janmabhoomi: from Prayagraj on the Grand Chord corridor to Ayodhya.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Diesel. DERIVATION INTEREST: Ayodhya is X001 - expect gray/x-transit, no pilgrimage_keep applied (Laurie reviews case-by-case). Junctions with RL-022 at Prayagraj.

lines.name: Astana–Almaty (Kazakh trunk)
lines.note: The length of the steppe: from the new capital at Astana south across the flat grassland to Karaganda, over the Betpak-Dala desert's edge, to Almaty under the Tian Shan.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Catenary. Runs (R109/R103 — the steppe and southern city are both kept). Astana → C433; Almaty → C432.

lines.name: Almaty–Shymkent–Tashkent (Silk Road corridor)
lines.note: Southwest along the old Silk Road: Almaty through Taraz to Shymkent, and across the border into Uzbekistan to Tashkent.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Catenary. Runs (R103 throughout — Kazakh south and Uzbek capital both recovered). Cross-border, genuinely through-linked (unlike the US-Mexico case) — historic Silk Road route, operating international service today.

lines.name: Afrosiyob (Tashkent–Samarkand–Bukhara)
lines.note: Uzbekistan's high-speed spine to the ancient cities: Tashkent west across the plain to Samarkand of Timur's blue domes, and on to Bukhara of the minarets.
lines.research_note: Principal roster (the real, operating Afrosiyob HSR). Tier B. Catenary. Runs (R103 recovered throughout).

lines.name: Sharq (Tashkent–Namangan, Fergana Valley)
lines.note: Over the mountains to the valley: Tashkent through the Kamchik Pass tunnel into the Fergana Valley, Kokand, and Namangan among the cotton fields.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Diesel. Runs (R103 — the Fergana Valley is recovered and densely populated).

lines.name: Bishkek–Balykchy (Kyrgyz line)
lines.note: East from the capital to the great lake: Bishkek across the Chuy valley to Balykchy on the shore of Issyk-Kul.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Diesel. Runs (R103). Bishkek → C409.

lines.name: Dushanbe–Qurghonteppa (Tajik line)
lines.note: South from the capital into the Vakhsh valley: Dushanbe through the mountains to Qurghonteppa's cotton country.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Diesel. Runs (R103). Dushanbe → C400.

lines.name: Trans-Caspian (Ashgabat–Türkmenbaşy)
lines.note: West across the Karakum to the Caspian: Ashgabat, the desert crossing, and Türkmenbaşy on the sea, gateway to the ferry crossing toward the Caucasus.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Diesel. DERIVATION: the whole run is X011 (Turkmen desert/aridity) — expect the line to sever whole, like Panama; kept on roster grayed. Ashgabat → C313 (contracting, consistent with X011).

lines.name: Israel Railways Coastal (Nahariya–Haifa–Tel Aviv–Ashkelon)
lines.note: The length of the coastal plain: Nahariya near the Lebanese border, Haifa on its bay, Tel Aviv's central hub, and south to Ashkelon.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Catenary. Runs (R079 recovered). Tel Aviv → C308.

lines.name: Tel Aviv–Jerusalem (fast line)
lines.note: Up through the Judean hills: Tel Aviv, Ben Gurion Airport, and the climb to Jerusalem.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Catenary. Runs (R079). Jerusalem the roster endpoint.

lines.name: Israel Railways Beersheba Line (Tel Aviv–Beersheba)
lines.note: South into the Negev: Tel Aviv through Lod and Kiryat Gat to Beersheba, gateway to the desert.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Catenary. Runs (R079/R078 recovered Negev override).

lines.name: RAI Tehran–Mashhad
lines.note: East across the plateau to the great shrine city: Tehran through Semnan and Shahrud, to Mashhad and the shrine of Imam Reza.
lines.research_note: Principal roster (real operating corridor, HSR upgrade underway). Tier B. Catenary. Runs (R082 recovered throughout). Tehran → C323 (contracting, zone governs → runs); Mashhad → C319.

lines.name: RAI Tehran–Qom–Isfahan–Shiraz
lines.note: South across the plateau: Tehran, Qom of the shrine, Isfahan of the great square, and Shiraz of the poets and gardens.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Catenary. Runs (R082 throughout). Isfahan → C317 (contracting, zone governs → runs).

lines.name: RAI Tehran–Tabriz
lines.note: Northwest to Azerbaijan: Tehran through Zanjan to Tabriz beneath its bazaar.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Catenary. Runs (R082 recovered).

lines.name: RAI Tehran–Ahvaz–Bandar Abbas (Gulf line)
lines.note: South to the Gulf: Tehran down through Qom and Ahvaz in the Khuzestan lowland, to Bandar Abbas on the Strait of Hormuz.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Catenary. DERIVATION: Tehran/Qom (R082) hold, but Ahvaz and Bandar Abbas are X008 (Gulf wet-bulb heat, same code as the UAE) — expect the southern end to gray/dead-tail. The Gulf coast dies on both the Iranian and Arabian shores alike.

lines.name: Etihad Rail passenger (Ghuweifat–Abu Dhabi–Dubai–Sharjah–Fujairah)
lines.note: The UAE's first national passenger railway, opened 2026: from Ghuweifat at the Saudi border, along the coast through Abu Dhabi and Dubai, Sharjah, and across to Fujairah on the Gulf of Oman.
lines.research_note: Principal roster (REAL, currently operating — phased launch June 2026 Abu Dhabi-Fujairah, expanding through Dubai/Sharjah/Al Dhafra by March 2027; confirmed via live search 2026-07-21). Tier B. Catenary. DERIVATION: the entire UAE resolves to X008 (Gulf wet-bulb heat) — expect the whole line to sever, like Panama and the Turkmen desert line. A brand-new national railway, lost to the heat it was built into. Dubai/Abu Dhabi are already C316/C312 (contracting/excluded), consistent.

lines.name: Saudi–Qatar HSR (Riyadh–Doha, planned)
lines.note: The planned Gulf high-speed link: Riyadh across the desert and the causeway approaches to Doha.
lines.research_note: Principal roster (real signed agreement, ~6-year build horizon as of 2026). Tier C (planned). Catenary. DERIVATION: Riyadh is X007 (Saudi interior heat/aridity), Doha is X008 (Gulf wet-bulb) — both ends are exclusion zones; expect the whole line to sever, like the UAE and Turkmen cases. A Gulf railway planned into a future its own model says the desert takes back.

lines.name: ENR Green Line HSR (Cairo–Alexandria)
lines.note: The new electric spine of the delta: Cairo out through Wadi El Natroun, to Alexandria on the Mediterranean — the first phase of Egypt's high-speed network.
lines.research_note: Principal roster (REAL, under construction — trial runs scheduled autumn 2026, confirmed via live search 2026-07-21). Tier C (planned/testing). Catenary. DERIVATION: the whole Nile Delta is X003 (heat/water-stress) — expect the line to sever whole, like Panama and the UAE. Cairo/Alexandria both C-status roster.

lines.name: ENR Nile Valley (Cairo–Luxor–Aswan)
lines.note: Up the river of the pharaohs: Cairo south through Asyut and Sohag, Luxor of the temples, and Aswan by the first cataract.
lines.research_note: Principal roster (the historic main line, real and operating today). Tier B. Diesel. DERIVATION: the entire Nile Valley corridor is X003 — expect the whole line to sever, one of Africa's oldest railways lost to the heat, like Panama and Turkmenistan.

lines.name: NRC Lagos–Ibadan
lines.note: The Chinese-built standard-gauge line: Lagos out through Agbado and the Ogun towns, to Ibadan in the southwest.
lines.research_note: Nigeria's busiest rail line (2026, expiry-tagged). Principal roster (REAL, operating since June 2021, ~2M+ riders, expanding to full 16-trains/day service in 2026 — confirmed via live search). Tier B. Diesel. DERIVATION: Lagos is X005 (coastal wet-bulb/lagoon heat), Ibadan is R068 (recovered interior) — expect the Lagos end to die, Ibadan to survive. The coast and the interior split.

lines.name: NRC Abuja–Kaduna
lines.note: The line that opened Nigeria's standard-gauge era: Abuja north through the middle belt to Kaduna.
lines.research_note: Principal roster (REAL, operating since 2016, the first modern Nigerian standard-gauge line). Tier B. Diesel. DERIVATION: Abuja is R068 (recovered federal capital), Kaduna is X005 — expect the Kaduna end to die, Abuja to survive. Same coast/Sahel-vs-interior split as Lagos-Ibadan.

lines.name: NRC Warri–Itakpe
lines.note: The line from the Delta ports to the iron country: Warri through the mangrove creeks to Itakpe and the Ajaokuta steel complex.
lines.research_note: Principal roster (REAL, operating since 2020). Tier B. Diesel. DERIVATION: Warri is X005 (Niger Delta coast), Itakpe/Ajaokuta are R068 (recovered interior) — same coastal/interior split again; the pattern holds a third time across Nigeria.

lines.name: TER Dakar–Diamniadio–Aéroport
lines.note: Senegal's flagship commuter line: Dakar's centre out to the new city at Diamniadio and Blaise Diagne International Airport.
lines.research_note: Principal roster (REAL, fully operational since 2021, phase 2 to the airport 2026, ~115,000 daily riders — confirmed via live search). Tier B. Catenary. Runs (R080 recovered).

lines.name: ONCF Al Boraq extension (Casablanca–Marrakech)
lines.note: South from the coast to the red city: Casablanca down through Settat to Marrakech beneath the Atlas.
lines.research_note: Principal roster (extends the existing Tangier–Casablanca line). Tier B. Catenary. Runs (R071 recovered).

lines.name: ONCF Fès–Oujda
lines.note: East to the Algerian border: Fès of the medina, Taza in the gap, and Oujda near the frontier.
lines.research_note: Principal roster. Tier B. Catenary. Runs (R045/R071 recovered).

lines.name: SNTF Algiers–Oran
lines.note: West along the Tell: Algiers, Blida, Relizane, to Oran on the sea.
lines.research_note: Principal roster (real operating line). Tier B. Diesel. Runs (R045 recovered).

lines.name: SNCFT Tunis–Sfax
lines.note: South along the coast: Tunis, Sousse of the medina, and Sfax of the olives.
lines.research_note: Principal roster (real operating line). Tier B. Diesel. Runs (R045 recovered).

lines.name: SNCC Lubumbashi–Kolwezi
lines.note: Into the copperbelt's heart: Lubumbashi through Likasi to Kolwezi and the great mines.
lines.research_note: Principal roster (real, historic). Tier B. Diesel. Runs (R064 recovered — junctions with RL-057 TAZARA at Lubumbashi's network).

lines.name: SNCC Kinshasa–Matadi
lines.note: The Congo's oldest railway: Kinshasa down to Matadi, the ocean port at the head of the Congo estuary.
lines.research_note: Principal roster (real, historic, 1898). Tier B. Diesel. Runs (R065 Bas-Congo recovered).

lines.name: Kenya Railways Nairobi–Naivasha (SGR extension)
lines.note: The extension into the Rift: Nairobi out past Kikuyu to Naivasha on its lake.
lines.research_note: Principal roster (real, opened 2023, extends the Madaraka Express SGR). Tier B. Catenary. Runs (R061 recovered). Junctions with RL-054 at Nairobi.
